ANNOTATION
This document “OMS-CLOUD 4.0. Special software. Programmer’s guide” RU
15861920.620111-04 33 07 is intended to introduce the functions of application programming
interface of special software (SSW) of automated system “OMS-CLOUD 4.0” (OMS-Cloud 4.0
AS).

## Page 11


11
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

1. PROGRAM PURPOSE
1.1.
Program Purpose
The specialized software is intended to enable the performance of target functions of
OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS including:
1) Ordering emission of marking codes.
2) Provision of emitted marking codes for application to products.
3) Verification of MC application.
1) Aggregation of ready and packed products with applied identification means (IM).
2) Writing-off of rejected products.
1.2.
Program Functions
OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS SSW enables the following functions:
1) Obtaining orders and providing emitted marking codes.
2) Obtaining notifications on the use (application) of marking codes and recording
marking code status in the Goods Group System.
3) Obtaining notifications on the aggregation of marking codes and recording packed
products aggregation in the Goods Group System.
4) Obtaining notifications on the rejection of marking codes and rejected products
registration in the Goods Group System.


---

## Page 12


12
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

2. PROGRAM APPLICATION ENVIRONMENT
2.1.
Applied Hardware
2.1.1. Required characteristics of PC for the operation of software component from OMS-
Cloud 4.0 AS SSW executed at OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS SSW HWC
In order to operate a software component from OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS SSW executed at
OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS SSW HWC, the following hardware (HW) shall be used:
1) CPU: Intel x86 architecture, clock rate of 3 GHz;
2) Platform: 64-bit;
3) RAM capacity: 16 Gb;
4) Hard-disk drive: 100 GB;
5) Network interface: Ethernet 100 Mbit/s.
Minimum network interface settings for OMS:
1) 1 static IP address;
2) Netmask;
3) Default gateway;
4) DNS settings.
Access to OMS-server:
1) In firewall, SSH-port shall be open (22 by default or another configured by network
administrator), as well as ports for Web-console of Imagenarium software and Web-
interface of OMS-software.
In order to ensure fail-safety, it is recommended to use three clustered physical or virtual
machines. Machines shall have identical configuration. All machines shall have the same SSH-
ports.
2.1.2. Required characteristics of PC to use “Web-Interface Module” software component
from OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS SSW
To operate the information services provided by OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS SSW, the following
hardware (HW) shall be used:
1) Non-shared HW:
− user AWS with the functional role of “OMS Operator / Administrator” (OMS Operator /
Administrator AWS) that is a PC with characteristics relevant to the recommended
requirements of Microsoft Windows OS 8.1 and later editions, and a network adapter
providing information-and-communication channel with HWC, OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS.


---

## Page 13


13
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

2.2.
Software required for program functioning
2.2.1. Software required for the functioning of software component from OMS-Cloud 4.0
AS SSW executed at OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS SSW HWC
In order to use the system, the server / virtual machine shall have the following basic
programs and components installed:
1) Operating system Linux CentOS 7;
2) SSH Server (authentication mode by name and password);
3) Command-line utility and system program packages: (bash, ifconfig, sysctl, curl, yum,
systemctl, yum-config-manager, unzip).
In order to install OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS SSW, operating system Linux CentOS 7 shall be
installed, and SSH Server, command-line utility and system program packages, as listed above
shall be set up.
2.2.2. Software required for the functioning of “Web-Interface Module” software
component from OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS SSW
To operate the information services provided by “Web-Interface Module” software
component from OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS SSW, the following general software components are
required:
1) Microsoft Windows OS 8.1 and later editions;
2) Web-browser:
− Mozilla Firefox 40 and later versions.
− Internet Explorer 9 and later versions.
− Google Chrome 37 and later versions.
2.3.
Software required for program operation
2.3.1. General requirements to request signing
In order to ensure legal value of sent messages by means of OMS API 3.0 interface,
optional parameter X-Signature has been added to the HTTP-header of request to OMS to include
a detached digital signature of request data (GCP’s detached signature is included into HTTP-
header within X-Signature parameter, in Base64 encoding).
Signature format is a detached signature in CMS-format. The Cryptographic Message
Syntax, CMS [IETF RFC 5652], is used for digital signature, hashing, authentication and arbitrary
message encryption. CMS utilizes encryption algorithms according to standards GOST 28147-
89, GOST R 34.10-2012 and GOST 34.11-2012, as well as to the document of Technical
Committee “Cryptographic Data Security” (TK 26), ““Information Technology. Cryptographic Data


---

## Page 14


14
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Security. Standardization Recommendations. Use of Algorithms of GOST 28147-89, GOST R
34.11 and GOST R 34.10 in CMS-Format Cryptographic Messages”.
CMS-values are generated by means of ASN.1 language as per GOST R ISO/IEC 8824-
1 using basic encoding rules (BER) as per GOST R ISO/IEC 8825-1.
For testing purposes, a test Certifying Center LLC CRYPTO-PRO will be supported
(https://www.cryptopro.ru/certsrv/).
The circulation participant generates a request, signs it and generates a detached
signature using GCP-certificate. The GCP's detached signature is included into HTTP-header
within X-Signature parameter, in Base64 encoding. Then, circulation participant sends the
request to Operator’s System.
Data Used to Sign HTTP-Requests:
1) For HTTP-message of GET-type, the signature will utilize the path, including request
string parameters (REQUEST_PATH + QUERY STRING, e.g. /ordrs?param=..),
without consideration of host-name, port or HTTP-headers;
2) For HTTP message of POST type, signature uses data posted within the body.
When OMS, within an HTTP request, receives “X-Signature” parameter containing
a detached signature of request and/or message-body parameters, it runs signature validation
and, if the signature is valid, it processes the request, otherwise the document will be rejected.
You can see an example of detached digital signature included in request in Table 1.
Note: the system does not support attached signature, therefore error and HTTP code
413 are returned when specifying this signature in “X-Signature” parameter.


---

2.3.2. Generating GS1 DataMatrix
In accordance with the requirements of GS1 General Specification, correct generation of
GS1 DataMatrix requires insertion of indication of symbols—ASCII232 in the beginning of the
obtained marking code string prior to conversion to DataMatrix; otherwise, technical means will
not recognize the code correctly and will be unable to process it correctly. Links to specifications
are given below:


---

## Page 16


16
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

1) GS1
General
Specification
(https://www.gs1.org/docs/barcodes/GS1_General_Specifications.pdf);
2) GS1
DataMatrix
Guideline
(https://www.gs1.org/docs/barcodes/GS1_DataMatrix_Guideline.pdf).
2.3.3. Requirements JSON-format processing
As the marking code contains special symbols, circulation participant, while integrating
their solutions, shall correctly process JSON-format with tools compliant with RFC 8259, and
never process it as Plain Text. The use of tools compliant with RFC 8259 within such solutions
ensures marking codes with special symbols are transferred and obtained correctly (special
symbols are screened).
Likewise, if circulation participants use XML format in their solutions, the special symbols
contained in a string shall be converted in accordance with XML specifications.

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4. PROGRAM CALL
4.1.
Introduction
API REST controller authenticates clients using the so-called client token sent by client
in HTTP-request header. The security marker (ClientToken) is sent in client token HTTP-header
“clientToken”.
Some API-methods use HTTP POST method during data submission. In such cases,
additional HTTP-header indication “Content-Type: application/json” shall be used.
OMS API methods use OMS-identifier “omsId” as parameters; OMS-identifier “omsId” is
available in OMS-settings.
For valid MC-symbols, see Table 3. These symbols shall be used in the following groups
of marking codes data: “Serial Number”, “Key Identifier”, “Verification Code”.
The API REST controller authenticates users with the help of so-called token sent by a
user in the HTTP request header. The security marker (token) is transmitted in the HTTP header
in the “clientToken” parameter (it can be transferred in the “Authorization” parameter for the
“Medicines for human use” goods group).
Table 3 – Valid MC-Symbols
Valid MC-symbols. Valid characters IC
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789!"%&'()*+,-
./_:;=<>?
4.2.
OMS-Method Call Sequences
The sequence of OMS-method call during the creation of a new MC-order is as follows:
1) Create marking code emission order (see cl. 4.4.1).
2) Obtain MC-array status from the order (see cl. 4.4.2).
3) Obtain MC from the order (see cl. 4.4.3).
4) Send report on MC-utilization (see cl. 4.4.11).
5) Send report on MC-aggregation (see cl. 4.4.10).
6) Send report on MC-disposal / rejection (see cl. 4.4.8).
For the diagram of OMS-method call sequence, see Figure 1.
The OMS API also provides auxiliary methods:
1) Obtain the report processing status (see section 4.4.13).
2) Close the order/suborder for the GTIN specified (see section 4.4.8).
3) Check availability of the OMS (see section 4.4.25).
4) Obtain a list of marking code package identifiers (see section  4.4.5).


---

## Page 20


20
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

5) Re-obtain marking codes from a marking code order (see section 4.4.6).
Notes.
1. In case there are any unused marking codes when the order is closed, a report on the
marking code cancellation will be generated and sent.
2. An order or suborder should be closed immediately after receiving the MCs, since if a
goods circulation participant does not close it, it will be automatically closed by the system
only after 48 hours (provided that all MCs have been taken from a suborder). This may
result in blocking of creation of new orders, as a goods circulation participant has a limit
of 100 active orders.

OMS-Method Call Sequence
Figure 1

## Page 21


21
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.3.
Description of MC-Emission Process
This subsection provides general description of marking code emission process. General
MC-emission process includes four key stages:
1) “01.01.00.00 Create MC-Emission Order”;
2) “01.02.00.00 Obtain MC-Array Status from Order”;
3) “01.03.00.00 Obtain MC from Order”;
4) “01.04.00.00 Send Report on MC-Utilization”.
The process of sending reports on aggregation and disposal / rejection is similar to
the process “01.04.00.00 Send Report on MC-Utilization”.
4.3.1. Process “01.01.00.00 Create MC-Emission Order”
For the diagram of MC-emission order creation process, see Figure 2.

Process “Create MC-Emission Order”
Figure 2
Description:
1) APCS generates an order and sends it to OMS;
2) OMS verifies the request and sends order to Emission Registrar;
3) Emission Registrar generates a request containing the MC-emission order and sends
it to Emission Server;


---

## Page 22


22
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4) Upon obtaining request containing MC-emission order, Emission Server verifies
the request.
− In case of any errors within the request, Emission Server registers them in a log;
− Emission Server generates an error message and sends it to Emission Registrar;
− Moving to Step 8) of main scenario.
5) In case of no errors, Emission Server verifies MC-emission order.
− In case of any errors within the request, Emission Server registers them in a log;
− Emission Server generates an error message and sends it to Emission Registrar;
− Moving to Step 8) of main scenario.
6) In case of no errors within the MC-emission order, Emission Server assigns
an identifier to the order and calculates order completion time;
− Emission Server sends the order for processing (the action is asynchronous);
7) Emission Server generates a response message and sends it to Emission Registrar;
8) Emission Registrar verifies request processing result;
9) Emission Registrar performs check for errors:
− In case of any errors within the message, Emission Server registers them in a log;
− Emission Registrar generates an error message and sends it to OMS;
− Moving to Step 12) of main scenario.
10) In case of no errors, Emission Registrar generates an empty MC-pool;
− Emission Registrar waits for the order completion time and requests emitted MC from
Emission Server (the action is asynchronous).
11) Emission Registrar sends a response message to OMS;
12) OMS obtains request processing result from Emission Registrar;
13) OMS performs check for errors;
− In case of any errors, OMS registers them in a log;
− OMS generates an error message and sends it to APCS;
− Moving to Step 16) of main scenario.
14) In case of no errors, OMS creates an MC-array;
− OMS waits for the order completion time and requests emitted MC from Emission
Registrar (the action is asynchronous).
15) OMS generates a response message and sends it to APCS;
16) APCS obtains request processing result from OMS;
17) APCS performs check for errors;
− In case of any errors, APCS registers them in a log;


---

## Page 23


23
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

− The process is terminated.
18) In case of no errors, APCS saves order data;
− APCS initiates process 01.02.00.00 “Obtain MC-Array Status from Order” (the action is
asynchronous).
19) The process is terminated.


---

## Page 24


24
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.3.2. Process “01.02.00.00 Obtain MC-Array Status from Order”
For the diagram of MC-array status obtainment process, see Figure 3.

Process “Obtain MC-Array Status from Order”
Figure 3
Description:
1) APCS waits for order completion time;
2) APCS generates a request to obtain MC-array status and sends it to OMS;
3) OMS verifies the request;
4) OMS performs check for errors:
− In case of any errors within the request, OMS registers them in a log;
− OMS generates an error message and sends it to APCS;
− Moving to Step 7 of main scenario.
5) OMS obtains information on MC-array;


---

## Page 25


25
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

6) OMS generates a response message and sends it to APCS;
7) APCS receives response message;
8) APCS performs check for errors:
− In case of any errors within the request, APCS registers them in a log;
− The process is terminated.
9) In case of no errors, APCS checks that MC-buffer status is “PENDING”:
− In case the MC-buffer status is “PENDING”, APCS initiates repeated request for MC-
array status.
− Moving to Step 1 of main scenario.
10)  In case of no errors, APCS checks that MC-buffer status is “ACTIVE”:
− In case MC-buffer status is not “ACTIVE”, the process is terminated
11) In case MC-buffer status is “ACTIVE”, APCS initiates process 01.03.00.00 “Obtain
MC from Order” (the action is asynchronous);
12) The process is terminated.


---

## Page 26


26
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.3.3. Process “01.03.00.00 Obtain MC from Order”
For the diagram of MC obtainment from business order process, see Figure 4.

Process “Obtain MC from Order”
Figure 4
Description:
1) APCS generates a request to obtain MC from business order and sends it to OMS;
2) OMS verifies the request;
3) OMS performs check for errors:
− In case of any errors within the request, OMS registers them in a log;
− OMS generates an error message and sends it to APCS;
− Moving to Step 6 of main scenario.
4) OMS generates an array of emitted MC;
5) OMS generates a response message and sends it to APCS;
6) APCS receives response message;
7) APCS performs check for errors:


---

## Page 27


27
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

− In case of any errors within the request, APCS registers them in a log;
− The process is terminated.
8) APCS processes the received message.
9) APCS checks for any other MC in the order.
− In case there are no other MC in the order, the process is terminated.
10) In case the order contains other MC, APCS checks whether the remaining MC should
be downloaded.
− In case the MC remaining in the order should be downloaded, APCS re-initiates
process 01.03.00.00 “Obtain MC from Order”;
− Moving to Step 1 of main scenario.
11) In case the remaining MC do not have to be downloaded, the process is terminated.


---

## Page 28


28
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.3.4. Process “01.04.00.00 Send Report on MC-Utilization”
For the diagram of MC-utilization report sending process, see Figure 5.

Process “Send Report on MC-Utilization”
Figure 5
Description:
1) APCS generates a request containing MC-utilization report and sends it to OMS;
2) OMS verifies the request and sends the request containing MC-utilization report to
Emission Registrar;
3) Emission Registrar generates a request containing the MC-utilization report and sends
it to Emission Server;
4) Upon obtaining request containing MC-utilization report, Emission Server verifies
the request.
− In case of any errors within the request, Emission Server registers them in a log;
− Emission Server generates an error message and sends it to Emission Registrar;
− Moving to Step 7 of main scenario.
5) In case of no errors, Emission Server assigns an identifier to the report;
6) Emission Server sends the report for processing to TT GIS (the action is
asynchronous);
7) Emission Server generates a response message and sends it to Emission Registrar;


---

## Page 29


29
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

8) Emission Registrar verifies request processing result;
9) Emission Registrar performs check for errors:
− In case of any errors within the message, Emission Server registers them in a log;
− Emission Registrar generates an error message and sends it to OMS;
− Moving to Step 10 of main scenario.
10) In case of no errors, Emission Registrar generates a response message and sends it
to OMS;
11) OMS obtains request processing result from Emission Registrar;
12) OMS performs check for errors;
− In case of any errors, OMS registers them in a log;
− OMS generates an error message and sends it to APCS;
− Moving to Step 13 of main scenario.
13) In case of no errors, OMS generates a response message and sends it to APCS;
14) APCS obtains request processing result from OMS;
15) APCS performs check for errors;
− In case of any errors, APCS registers them in a log;
− The process is terminated.
16) In case of no errors, APCS saves report identifier, the process is terminated.

## Page 30

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.4.
Methods of OMS API
A URL structure of the OMS API looks as follows: <url of environment>/api/v3/,
where <url of environment> – base address of the environment which hosts API.
The following OMS API addresses are available:
1) https://suz.sandbox.crptech.ru – new base address of the demo environment
(including “Medicines for human use” goods group).
2) https://suzgrid.crpt.ru – base address of the production environment.
3) https://api.suz.sandbox.crptech.ru – base address of the demo environment with
GOST TLS support.
4) https://api.suzgrid.crpt.ru – base address of the production environment with GOST
TLS support (pilot deployment stage), full-scale production use will begin in Q2 2027.
GOST TLS guarantees protection against data tampering and interception, and ensures
compliance with national requirements for information security.
Note: Methods of OMS-Cloud API are advised to be called no more than 100 times per
second for each “IP and omsId” pair (source). If the total number of calls for a single omsId
exceeds 100 requests per second (regardless of the number of the IP addresses used), the
source may be temporarily blocked.


---

## Page 31


31
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.4.1. Method “Create Marking Code Emission Order”
This method is used to create and send marking code emission orders.  Description on
how to receive the security marker is provided in section 9.
Notes:
1. One goods item (goods code, GTIN) in one order should not exceed 2 000 000 marking
codes.  If there are more than one MC in the order (goods code, GTIN), then a quantity
of the MCs should not exceed 150 000 marking codes for each goods code.
2. Maximum quantity of the goods codes in one MC order should not exceed 10, if several
goods codes are specified in one order, the goods codes should not be duplicated.
3. Quantity of the active orders cannot exceed 100 at once. Active orders include those
orders with READY status that have MC-buffer with ACTIVE, PENDING status.
A queue can also contain not more than 100 orders. These orders include orders with
statuses CREATED, PENDING, APPROVED.
If one of the limits is reached, no orders can be created.
4. This method can be called for each pair (“IP and omsId”) no more than 100 times per
second (for more details, see 4.4).

and “Titanium metal products” goods groups in connection with the fact that these goods groups
are not involved in the marking code distribution.
For description of JSON format of the “OrderProduct” object of the request for creation
and sending of the order for MC emission (“Order” object), see Table 8.

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.4.1.1.1 List of Attributes for “Tobacco Products” Goods Group
Example of the REST request for the “Tobacco products” goods group is shown in Figure
6.
POST /api/v3/order?omsId=CDF12109-10D3-11E6-8B6F-0050569977A1 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Content-Type: application/json
X-Signature: {подпись}

{
   "productGroup":"tobacco",
   "products":[{
"gtin":"01334567894339",
   "quantity": 2,
   "serialNumberType": "SELF_MADE",
   "serialNumbers":[ "Z9bmNYR", "8i8PjF3"],
   "templateId":3,
   "cisType": "GROUP",
"attributes": {
"mrp":"31055"
}
}],
   "attributes": {
      "factoryId":"Identifier",
      "factoryName":"Tobacco Fac",
      "factoryAddress":"Address",
      "factoryCountry":"Country",
      "productCode":"6789",
      "productDescription":"Simple ",
      "poNumber":"12345",
      "releaseMethodType": "IMPORT",
      "approvalNumber": "AN-1",
      "expectedStartDate":"2019-03-01"
      }
}
Example of the REST request for the “Tobacco products” goods group
Figure 6
A possibility of goods code-sharing has been excluded for the “Tobacco products”,
“Nicotine products” and “Alternative tobacco products” goods groups. An order will be rejected if
a goods group of the order does not correspond to the goods group (of the goods code) specified
in the goods card in the NC.
A possibility of emission is checked in line with licenses for consumer and group packages
when ordering MCs for the “Tobacco products” goods group. If a GCP has a valid license, the
check will succeed. If there is no valid license, the order will be rejected.
A description of the parameters which are transferred in the “attributes” attribute of the
“Order” object for the “Tobacco products” goods group can be found in Table 10.

## Page 41


41
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Table 11 – Structure of the attributes parameter of the “OrderProduct” object for
the “Tobacco products” goods group
Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
mrp
Maximum retail price
“mrp” field (Maximum retail price) is
obligatory, a maximum retail price shall be
indicated in kopecks, up to ones, e.g., if the
price is 105 rubles and 1 kopeck, that makes
10501. For a block this is the sum of all
packs, also in kopecks.
A value of the “Maximum retail price”
parameter shall correspond to the allowed
range specified in the GCP’s profile and
shall be greater than the Single minimum
price (the SMP). From January 1, 2026 to
December 31, 2026, the SMP will be 153
rubles.
String
(4–6)
Yes

A description of the parameters which are transferred in the “attributes” attribute of the
“OrderProduct” object for the “Light industry” goods group can be found in Table 13.
Table 13 – Structure of the attributes parameter of the “OrderProduct” object for
the “Light industry” goods group
Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
producer

INN of the goods card owner
It is specified only if releaseMethodType =
“REMARK” or “REMAINS”.
A period of availability of the REMAINS is
specified in a note of the description of the
guide “Method of Goods Introduction into
Circulation” (See subsection 5.3.1.1).
String
No
Conditionally
mandatory
Notes:
1. Remarking and marking of the remaining items are available for the original gtin if a
goods card belongs to a participant that has sent the order or has been granted access
through the mechanism of the sub-account.
2. Remarking and marking of the remaining items are available for the original gtin (local
range – 046 and 004 or global one – not belonging to ranges 046, 004 and 029) if a goods
circulation participant is not the goods card owner or does not have access by sub-
account. In this case, INN of the goods card owner should be specified in a request
structure in the “producer” parameter.
3. Marking of remaining items is available for cards with a short set of attributes for the
technical range (029) and the “UNIT” marking code type, but only during the third payment
wave.
4. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of remarking or marking of the remaining items), there should be no serial
numbers in a request structure. When the system generates serial numbers, the first
symbol is the State identifier used to mark goods outside production.

4.4.1.1.6 List of Attributes for “Photo Cameras (Except Cine Cameras), Flash Lights and
Flash Bulbs” Goods Group
For REST request example for the “Photo cameras (except cine cameras), flash lights and
flash bulbs” goods group, see Figure 11.

Notes:
1. Emission of the marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment upon
emission”) is available for the GTIN fee implementation groups upon the relevant date.
When paymentType = 1 ("Payment upon emission") is specified, gtin from different fee
implementation groups can be specified in the order, if on that date they have become
available for payment upon emission. GTIN fee implementation group can be determined
in a goods card:
− GTIN of the first fee implementation group is available from June 1, 2021.
− GTIN of the second fee implementation group is available from September 1, 2021.
− GTIN of the third fee implementation group is available from December 1, 2021.
− GTIN of the fourth fee implementation group will be available from November 1,
2026.


---

## Page 54


54
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

− GTIN of the fifth fee implementation group will be available from July 1, 2026.

2. Emission of marking codes with payment type (“paymentType”) = 2 (“Payment upon
application”) for:
− Group 4 GTINs included in the pilot program will be available from January 30, 2026
to October 31, 2026;
3. An attribute of the “Farmer” is supported for the “Dairy products” goods group based
on the All-Russia Classifier of Organizational Legal Forms, defining a "Farmer" category
of the goods circulation participant. For the “Farmer” category, the “Payment upon
emission” and “Payment upon application” are available for GTINs of the first, second and
the third fee implementation groups from September 1, 2024, the “Payment upon
emission” will be available for GTINs of the fourth fee implementation group from
November 1, 2026.

## Page 56


56
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Table 23 – Structure of the attributes parameter of the “OrderProduct” object for
the “Bicycles and bicycle frames” goods group
Field
Description
Type
Mandatory

It

Notes:
1. Emission of the marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment upon
emission”) is available for the GTIN fee implementation groups upon the relevant date.
GTIN fee implementation group can be determined in a goods card:
— GTIN of the first fee implementation group is available from June 1, 2024.
2. Remarking and marking of goods outside production or import are available for the
original gtin if a goods card belongs to a participant who has sent the order or has been
granted access through the mechanism of the sub-account.
3. Remarking and marking of goods outside production or import are available for the
original gtin (local range – 046 and 004 or global one – not belonging to ranges 046, 004
and 029) if a goods circulation participant is not the goods card owner or does not have
access by means of sub-account. In this case, INN of the goods card owner should be
specified in a request structure in the “producer” parameter.
4. Marking of goods outside production or import is unavailable for technical gtin (range
029).
5. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of remarking or marking of goods outside production or import), there should be
no serial numbers in a request structure. When the system generates serial numbers, the
first symbol is the State identifier used to mark goods outside production.
6. Emission with a method of release of goods into circulation - “REAPPLY” is available
only for consumer packages “cisType” = UNIT.

---

## Page 59


59
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

1.  Emission of the marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment
upon emission”) is available for the GTIN fee implementation groups upon the relevant
date. When paymentType = 1 ("Payment upon emission") is specified, gtin from different
fee implementation groups can be specified in the order, if on that date they have become
available for payment upon emission. GTIN fee implementation group can be determined
in a goods card:
⎯ GTIN of the first fee implementation group is available from October 1, 2023.
⎯ GTIN of the third fee implementation group is available from October 1, 2024.
⎯ GTIN of the fifth fee implementation group is available from September 1, 2024.
⎯ GTIN of the sixth fee implementation group is available from October 1, 2025.
⎯ GTIN of the seventh fee implementation group will be available from September 1,
2026.
⎯ GTIN of the tenth fee implementation group will be available from February 1,
2026.
2. Emission of the marking codes with payment type (“paymentType”) = 2 (“Payment
upon application”) for:
— Group 2 GTINs included in the pilot program is available from October 15, 2023 to
December 31, 2025;
— Group 4 GTINs included in the pilot program is available from September 1, 2024 to
August 31, 2026;
— Group 7 GTINs included in the pilot program is available from the first quarter of 2026;
— Group 8 GTINs included in the pilot program is available from April 29, 2026 to February
29, 2027.
3. Remarking and marking of remaining items are available for the original gtin if a goods
card belongs to a participant that has sent the order or has been granted access through
the mechanism of the sub-account.
4. Remarking and marking of remaining items are available for the original gtin (local
range – 046 and 004 or global one – not belonging to ranges 046, 004 and 029) if a goods
circulation participant is not the goods card owner or does not have access by sub-
account. In this case, INN of the goods card owner should be specified in a request
structure in the “producer” parameter.


---

## Page 60


60
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

5. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of remarking or marking of remaining items), there should be no serial numbers
in a request structure. When the system generates serial numbers, the first symbol is the
State identifier used to mark goods outside production.
6. Issuance for marking of remaining items is available for consumer package, group
package, and sets.

---

## Page 61


61
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

A possibility of goods code-sharing has been excluded for the “Tobacco products”,
“Nicotine products” and “Alternative tobacco products” goods groups. An order will be rejected if
a goods group of the order does not correspond to the goods group (of the goods code) specified
in the goods card in the NC.
A possibility of emission is checked in line with licenses for consumer and group packages
when ordering MCs for the “Alternative tobacco products” goods group. If a GCP has a valid
license, the check will succeed. If there is no valid license, the order will be rejected.

A description of the parameters which are transferred in the “attributes” attribute of the
“Order” object for the “Alternative tobacco products” goods group can be found in Table 26.

Note:
1) The “cisType” value is set for the following contidions:
- if “cisType”= UNIT , then permissible values of the “templateId” attribute are 14 and 15;
- if “cisType”= GROUP, then permissible value of the “templateId” attribute is 14.
2) If the value (>=0) is specified in the parameter of the maximum retail price (mrp), then,
when emitting a marking code by using a template templateId = 14, an MRP (AI 8005)
will be automatically filled in a structure of the marking code for each goods item and will
contain a digital format corresponding to 6 zeros – 000000. If the parameter is missing,
there will be no MRP (AI 8005) in a structure of the marking codes.
3) If a marking code is emitted by means of template templateId = 15, regardless of
availability of the filled parameter of the maximum retail price (mrp) or its lack, the MRP
will be automatically filled in a structure of the marking code for each goods item and will
contain a character format (in the recoded format, it will correspond to 4А – АААА).
A description of the parameters which are transferred in the “attributes” attribute of the
“OrderProduct” object for the “Alternative tobacco products” goods group can be found in Table
27.

implementation groups can be specified in the order, if on that date they have become available
for payment upon emission. GTIN fee implementation group can be determined in a goods card:
— GTINs of the first fee implementation group is available from December 1, 2021.
— GTINs of the second fee implementation group is available from March 1, 2022.
— GTINs of the third fee implementation group is available from September 1, 2023.

Notes:
1. Emission of the marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment upon
emission”) is available for the GTIN fee implementation groups upon the relevant date.
When paymentType = 1 ("Payment upon emission") is specified, gtin from different fee
implementation groups can be specified in the order, if on that date they have become
available for payment upon emission. GTIN fee implementation group can be determined
in a goods card:
— GTINs of the first fee implementation group is available from April 1, 2023.
— GTINs of the second fee implementation group is available from October 1, 2023.
— GTINs of the third fee implementation group is available from January 15, 2024.
2. Restrictions are imposed on emission of the marking codes of the “Beer, beer-based
and low-alcohol beverages” goods group. Participants that have “Wholesale trading” and
“Retail and another” roles can issue marking codes only with the use of the “REAPPLY”
(“Marking outside of production or import”) method of introduction into circulation.
Emission of the marking codes by using other methods of introduction into circulation is
unavailable for these participants categories.


---

## Page 67


67
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

A description of the parameters which are transferred in the “attributes” attribute of the
“OrderProduct” object for the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages” goods group can be
found in Table 30.
Table 30 – Structure of the attributes parameter of the “OrderProduct” object for
the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages” goods group
Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
producer
INN of the goods card owner
It is specified only if releaseMethodType =
“REAPPLY”.
String
(1-20)
No
Conditionally
mandatory

Notes:
1. Marking of the goods outside of production or import is available for the original gtin if
a goods card belongs to a participant who has sent the order or has been granted access
through the mechanism of the sub-account.
2. Marking of the goods outside of production or import is available for the original gtin
(local range – 046 and 004, or global one – not belonging to ranges 046, 004 and 029) if
a goods circulation participant is not a goods card owner or does not have access through
the sub-account. In this case, INN of the goods card owner should be specified in a
request structure in the “producer” parameter.
3. Marking of goods outside of production or import is unavailable for technical gtin (range
029).
4. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of marking of goods outside of production or import), there should be no serial
numbers in a request structure. When the system generates serial numbers, the first
symbol is the State identifier used to mark goods outside of production.
5. Emission with a method of release of goods into circulation - “REAPPLY” is available
only for consumer packages “cisType” = UNIT and with a payment type - Payment upon
emission (“paymentType” = 1).

Example of the REST request for the “Nicotine products” goods group
Figure 19
A possibility of goods code-sharing has been excluded for the “Tobacco products”,
“Nicotine products” and “Alternative tobacco products” goods groups. An order will be rejected if
a goods group of the order does not correspond to the goods group (of the goods code) specified
in the goods card in the NC.
A possibility of emission is checked in line with licenses for consumer and group packages,
as well as sets when ordering MCs for the “Nicotine products” goods group. If a GCP has a valid
license, the check will succeed. If there is no valid license, the order will be rejected.
A description of the parameters which are transferred in the “attributes” attribute of the
“Order” object for the “Nicotine products” goods group can be found in Table 31.

---

## Page 70


70
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

⎯ When emitting the MC for goods with marking codes template - templateId = 21 (blocks), the
maximum retail price (mrp) for each item is automatically filled in and contains a number format
corresponding to 6 zeros – 000000.
⎯ When emitting the MC for goods with marking codes template - templateId = 22 (packs), the
maximum retail price (mrp) for each item is automatically filled in and contains a character
format (in recoded form corresponding to 4А – АААА).

---

## Page 73


73
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

1. Emission of the marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment
upon emission”) is available for the GTIN fee implementation groups upon the relevant
date. When paymentType = 1 ("Payment upon emission") is specified, gtin from
different fee implementation groups can be specified in the order, if on that date they
have become available for payment upon emission. GTIN fee implementation group can
be determined in a goods card:
— GTINs of the first fee implementation group is available from October 1, 2023.
— GTINs of the second fee implementation group is available from June 1, 2024.
— GTINs of the third fee implementation group is available from September 1, 2025.
— GTINs of the fourth fee implementation group is available from March 31, 2026.
2. Emission of the marking codes with payment type (“paymentType”) = 2 (“Payment
upon application”) for:
— Group 3 GTINs included in the pilot program is available from June 1, 2025 to August
31, 2025.
3. Remarking, marking of remaining items and marking of goods outside production or
import are available for the original gtin if a goods card belongs to a participant that has
sent the order or has been granted access through the mechanism of the sub-account.
4. Remarking, marking of remaining items and marking of goods outside production or
import are available for the original gtin (local range – 046 and 004, or global one – not
belonging to ranges 046, 004 and 029) if a goods circulation participant is not the goods
card owner or does not have access by sub-account. In this case, INN of the goods card
owner should be specified in a request structure in the “producer” parameter.
5. Marking of goods outside production or import is unavailable for technical gtin (range
029).
6. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of remarking, marking of remaining items or marking of goods outside production
or import), there should be no serial numbers in a request structure. When the system
generates serial numbers, the first symbol is the State identifier used to mark goods
outside production.
7. Emission with a method of release of goods into circulation - “REAPPLY” is available
only for consumer packages “cisType” = UNIT.

the “Non-alcoholic beer” goods group
Field
Description

INN of the goods card owner
It is specified only if releaseMethodType =

Circulation” (See subsection 5.3.1.1).
String
No
Conditionally

Notes:
1. Emission of the marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment upon
emission”) is available for the GTIN fee implementation groups upon the relevant date.
GTIN fee implementation group can be determined in a goods card:
— GTINs of the first fee implementation group is available from October 1, 2024.
2. Remarking of the goods is available for the original gtin if a goods card belongs to a
participant who has sent the order or has been granted access through the mechanism
of the sub-account.
3. Remarking of the goods is available for the original gtin (local range – 046 and 004, or
global one – not belonging to ranges 046, 004 and 029) if a goods circulation participant
is not the goods card owner or does not have access through the mechanism of the sub-
account. In this case, INN of the goods card owner should be specified in a request
structure in the “producer” parameter.
4. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of remarking), there should be no serial numbers in a request structure. When
the system generates serial numbers, the first symbol is the State identifier used to mark
goods outside production.

Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
producer
INN of the goods card owner
It is specified only if releaseMethodType =

String
(1-20)
No
Conditionally

Notes:
1. Emission of the marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment upon
emission”) is available for the GTIN fee implementation groups upon the relevant date.
Fees will be introduced from December 1, 2025.
2. Marking of goods outside production or import is available for the original gtin if a goods
card belongs to a participant who has sent the order or has been granted access through
the mechanism of the sub-account.


---

## Page 88


88
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

3. Marking of goods outside production or import is available for the original gtin (local
range – 046 and 004, or global – not belonging to ranges 046, 004 and 029) if a goods
circulation participant is not the goods card owner or does not have access through the
mechanism of the sub-account. In this case, INN of the goods card owner should be
specified in a request structure in the “producer” parameter.
4. Marking of goods outside production or import is unavailable for technical gtin (range
029).
5. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of marking of goods outside production or import), serial numbers must not be
included in the request. When the system generates serial numbers, the first character is
the State identifier used to mark goods outside production.
6. Emission using the “REAPPLY” method of release of goods into circulation is available
only for consumer packaging with “cisType” = UNIT and with the payment type “Payment
upon emission” (“paymentType” = 1).

— GTIN of the third fee implementation group is available from October 1, 2025.
— GTIN of the fourth fee implementation group is available from December 1, 2025.
— GTIN of the fifth fee implementation group will be available from September 1, 2026.


---

## Page 99


99
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

2. Emission of the marking codes with payment type (“paymentType”) = 2 (“Payment
upon application”) for:
— Group 4 GTINs included in the pilot program is available from June 1, 2025 to August
31, 2025.
— Group 5 GTINs included in the pilot program is available from September 1, 2025 to
August 31, 2026.

A description of the parameters transferred in the “attributes” attribute of the
“OrderProduct” object for the “Cosmetics, household chemicals, and personal hygiene products”
goods group can be found in Table 50.
Table 50 – Structure of the attributes parameter of the “OrderProduct” object for
the “Cosmetics, household chemicals, and personal hygiene products” goods group
Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
producer
INN of the goods card owner
It is specified only if releaseMethodType =
“REAPPLY”.
String
(1-20)
No
Conditionally
mandatory

Notes:
1. Marking of the goods outside of production or import is available for the original gtin if
a goods card belongs to a participant who has sent the order or has been granted access
through the mechanism of the sub-account.
2. Marking of the goods outside of production or import is available for the original gtin
(local range – 046 and 004, or global one – not belonging to ranges 046, 004 and 029) if
a goods circulation participant is not the goods card owner or does not have access
through the mechanism of the sub-account. In this case, INN of the goods card owner
should be specified in a request structure in the “producer” parameter.
3. Marking of goods outside of production or import is unavailable for technical gtin (range
029).
4. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of marking of goods outside production or import), serial numbers must not be
included in the request. When the system generates serial numbers, the first symbol is
the State identifier used to mark goods outside production.
5. Emission with a method of release of goods into circulation - “REAPPLY” is available
only for consumer packages “cisType” = UNIT and with a payment type - Payment upon
emission (“paymentType” = 1).

Notes:
1. Emission of the marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment upon
emission”) is available for the GTIN fee implementation groups upon the relevant date.
When paymentType = 1 ("Payment upon emission") is specified, gtin from different fee
implementation groups can be specified in the order, if on that date they have become
available for payment upon emission. GTIN fee implementation group can be determined
in a goods card:
— GTIN of the first fee implementation group is available from December 1, 2024.
— GTIN of the second fee implementation group is available from March 1, 2025.
— GTIN of the third fee implementation group will be available from November 1, 2026.

2. Emission of the marking codes with payment type (“paymentType”) = 2 (“Payment
upon application”) for:
— Group 3 GTINs included in the pilot program will be available from January 30, 2026 to
October 31, 2026.
3. An attribute of the “Farmer” is supported for the “Canned foods” goods group based on
the All-Russia Classifier of Organizational Legal Forms, defining a "Farmer" category of
the goods circulation participant. The “Payment upon application” and “Payment upon
emission” are available for the “Farmer” category.

1. Emission of the marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment upon
emission”) is available for the GTIN fee implementation groups upon the relevant date.
GTIN fee implementation group can be determined in a goods card:
— GTIN of the first fee implementation group is available from October 1, 2024;
— GTIN of the second fee implementation group will be available from December 15,
2025.
2. Remarking is available for the original gtin if a goods card belongs to a participant who
has sent an order or has been granted access through the mechanism of the sub-account.
3. Remarking is available for the original gtin (local range – 046 and 004 or global one –
not belonging to ranges 046, 004 and 029) if a goods circulation participant is not a goods
card owner or does not have access by sub-account. In this case, INN of the goods card
owner shall be specified in a request structure in the “producer” parameter.
4. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of remarking), there should be no serial numbers in a request structure. When
the system generates serial numbers, the first symbol is the State identifier used to mark
goods outside production.

Notes:


---

## Page 121


121
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

1. Emission of the marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment upon
emission”) is available for the GTIN fee implementation groups upon the relevant date.
When paymentType = 1 ("Payment upon emission") is specified, gtin from different fee
implementation groups can be specified in the order, if on that date they have become
available for payment upon emission. GTIN fee implementation group can be determined
in a goods card:
— GTIN of the first fee implementation group is available from September 1, 2025.
2. Marking of remaining items and marking of goods outside production or import are
available for the original gtin if a goods card belongs to a participant who has sent the
order or has been granted access through the sub-account mechanism.
3. Marking of remaining items and marking of goods outside production or import are
available for the original gtin (local range – 046 and 004 or global one – not belonging to
ranges 046, 004 and 029) if a goods circulation participant is not the goods card owner
or does not have access through the sub-account mechanism. In this case, INN of the
goods card owner should be specified in a request structure in the “producer” parameter.
4. Marking of remaining items and marking of goods outside production or import are
unavailable for goods cards of the technical range (029).
5. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of marking of remaining items or marking of goods outside production or import),
there should be no serial numbers in a request structure. When the system generates
serial numbers, the first symbol is the State identifier used to mark goods outside
production.
6. Emission with a method of release of goods into circulation - “REAPPLY” is available
only for consumer packages “cisType” = UNIT and with a payment type - Payment upon
emission (“paymentType” = 1).

Notes:
1. Marking of goods outside production or import is available for the original gtin if a goods
card belongs to a participant who has sent the order or has been granted access through
the mechanism of the sub-account.
2. Marking of goods outside production or import is available for the original gtin (local
range - 046 and 004 or global – not belonging to ranges 046, 004 and 029) if a goods
circulation participant is not the goods card owner or does not have access through the
mechanism of the sub-account. In this case, the INN of the goods card owner must be
specified in the request structure in the “producer” parameter.
3. Marking of goods outside production or import is unavailable for technical gtin (range
029).
4. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of marking of goods outside production or import), serial numbers must not be
included in the request. When the system generates serial numbers, the first character is
the State identifier used to mark goods outside production.
5. Emission using the method of release of goods into circulation, “REAPPLY”, is
available only for consumer packages where “cisType” = UNIT.
6. Marking of goods outside production or import with the “Payment upon emission”
payment type will be available from May 1, 2026 (payment implementation start date).

Note: emission of marking codes with a payment type (paymentType = 1, “Payment upon
emission”) is available for GTIN payment groups upon the relevant date. The GTIN payment
group can be determined in a goods card:
— GTINs of the first payment group become available as of March 1, 2026.
A description of the parameters transferred in the “attributes” attribute of the
“OrderProduct” object for the “Real fur” goods group can be found in Table 76.


---

## Page 147


147
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Table 76 – Structure of the attributes parameter of the “OrderProduct” object for
the “Real fur” goods group
Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
producer
INN of the goods card owner
It is specified only if releaseMethodType =
“REAPPLY”.
String
(1-20)
No
Conditionally
mandatory

Notes:
1. Marking of goods outside production or import is available for the original gtin if a goods
card belongs to a participant who has sent the order or has been granted access through
the mechanism of the sub-account.
2. Marking of goods outside production or import is available for the original gtin (local
range – 046 and 004, or global – not belonging to ranges 046, 004 and 029) if a goods
circulation participant is not the goods card owner or does not have access through the
sub-account mechanism. In this case, the INN of the goods card owner must be specified
in a request structure in the “producer” parameter.
3. Marking of goods outside production or import is unavailable for technical gtin (range
029).
4. In case of emission of the marking codes for a goods card for which there is no permit
(as part of marking of goods outside production or import), serial numbers must not be
included in the request. When the system generates serial numbers, the first character is
the State identifier used to mark goods outside production.
5. Emission with a method of release of goods into circulation - “REAPPLY” is available
only for consumer packages “cisType” = UNIT.
6. Marking of goods outside production or import with the “Payment upon emission”
payment type will be available as of March 1, 2026, when fee liability takes effect.

4.4.1.2. Response to Request
Example of JSON response is shown in Figure 54.
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Pragma: no-cache
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
Expires: 0
X-Frame-Options: DENY
X-Content--Options: nosniff
Content-Type: application/json;charset=UTF-8
Content-Length: 111
Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, max-age=0, must-revalidate

{
"omsId":"CDF12109-10D3-11E6-8B6F-0050569977A1",
"orderId": "b024ae09-ef7c-449e-b461-
05d8eb116c79", "expectedCompleteTimestamp": 5100
}

Example of JSON Response
Figure 54
The method returns a unique order identifier and order expected completion time in
milliseconds (divide the result time by 1,000 for seconds, and by 60 for minutes). Value “orderId”
is used to obtain MC from the order upon its completion (see clause 4.4.3). For error codes, see
subsection 6.2.

4.4.2. Method “Get a status of MC array from the order”
This method is used to get the current status of the MC array from the order and requires
the following parameters: security marker (token), OMS identifier, “orderId” order identifier.
Description on how to receive a security marker is provided in section 9
4.4.2.1. Request
Example of REST request is shown in Figure 55.
GET /api/v3/order/status?orderId=b024ae09-ef7c-449e-b461-05d8eb116c79&omsId=cdf12109-10d3-11e6-8b6f-
0050569977a1&gtin=01334567894339 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f


---

Note: If an order has been rejected, rejectionReason field will contain “Order declined: “

---

## Page 156


156
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.4.3. Method “Obtain a List of Marking Code Orders”
This method is used to get a business orders status by using the following parameters:
security marker (token), OMS identifier. Description on how to receive a security marker is
provided in section 9.
Notes:
1. The method is intended for APCS recovery after a total data loss; use of its functional
capabilities during normal operating processes with the OMS is prohibited.
2. This method (as well as the method of creating orders) can be addressed from one
source not more than 100 times per second.
4.4.3.1. Request
Example of REST request is shown in Figure 59.
GET /api/v3/order/list?omsId=cdf12109-10d3-11e6-8b6f-0050569977a1 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Example of REST Request
Figure 59
REST request parameters can be found in Table 83.
Table 83 – REST request parameters
Parameter
Value
HTTP method
GET
URL
<url of the environment>/api/v3/order/list?omsId={omsId}

HTTP Header Parameters can be found in Table 84.


---

4.4.4. Method “Obtain MC from the MC order”
This method is used to obtain the MC array of the specific order by using the following
parameters: security marker (token), OMS identifier, order identifier, GTIN, quantity of the
requested codes. Description on how to receive a security marker is provided in section 9.
4.4.4.1. Request
Example of REST request is shown in Figure 61.
GET /api/v3/codes?orderId=b024ae09-ef7c-449e-b461-
05d8eb116c79&gtin=01334567894339&quantity=15&omsId=CDF12109-10D3-11E6-8B6F-0050569977A1 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f

Figure 61

&quantity={quantity}&gtin={gtin}

have been printed (See section 4.4.8).

4.4.5. Method “Obtain a List of Marking Code Package Identifiers”
This method is used to obtain a list of the marking code package identifiers issued previously
from the marking code order that are necessary for repeated request for marking codes by calling
a method “Re-obtain marking codes from marking code order” (See section 4.4.6).
The method uses the following parameters: OMS identifier and MC order identifier.
Description on how to receive a security marker is provided in section 9.
4.4.5.1. Restrictions
A list of previously issued marking code package identifiers may be obtained only if the
marking code suborder has not been closed and the first print request has been processed via
API.
4.4.5.2. Request
Example of request is shown in Figure 63.


---

163
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.4.6. Method “Re-obtain marking codes from marking code order”
This method is used to re-obtain emitted MC array from the marking code order in case
when marking codes were not obtained due to communications errors when interacting with the
OMS.


---

## Page 167


167
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

The method uses the following parameters: OMS identifier, marking code package identifier.
Description on how to receive a security marker is provided in section 9.
A list of previously issued marking code package identifiers is obtained by calling the method
“Obtain a List of Marking Code Package Identifiers” (See section 4.4.5).
4.4.6.1. Restrictions
Marking codes can be repeatedly requested only if
1) they have been previously requested via API;
2) marking code order has not been closed;
3) the method of goods release into circulation or the group of FEACN codes for which
the marking codes were issued was not closed and/or blocked. For example, the method
of goods release into circulation, “REMAINS” (Marking of remaining items), is available
only for a limited period and for certain groups of FEACN codes.
4.4.6.2. Request
Example of request is shown in Figure 65.

GET /api/v3/order/codes/retry?omsId=a024ae09-ef7c-449e-b461-05d8eb116c90&blockId=a024ae09-ef7c-449e-b461-
05d8eb116c90 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Request Example
Figure 65
REST request parameters can be found in Table 97.

of

environment>/api/v3/order/codes/retry?omsId={omsId}&blockId={blockId}

4.4.8. Method “Close MC order/suborder”
This method is used to close order by using the following parameters: security marker
(token), OMS identifier, order identifier. Description on how to receive a security marker is
provided in section 9.
4.4.8.1. Request
Example of REST request is shown in Figure 69.
POST /api/v3/order/close?omsId=cdf12109-10d3-11e6-8b6f-0050569977a1 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Content-Type: application/json
X-Signature: {signature}

{
"orderId":"b024ae09-ef7c-449e-b461-05d8eb116c79"
}

4.4.9. Method “Send Report on MC Disposal / Rejection”
This method is used to send report on MC disposal / rejection to OMS.  Description on
how to receive the security marker is provided in section 9
Note: currently, this method is available for the following goods groups:
⎯  "Tobacco Products”.
⎯ “Dairy products”. A structure of the request sent for the dairy products shall contain the
“DropoutReport” basic object without attributes in the “attributes” parameter for the goods
group.
⎯ “Packaged water”. A structure of the request sent for the packaged water shall contain the
“DropoutReport” basic object without attributes in the “attributes” parameter for the goods
group.
⎯ “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages”. A structure of the request sent for this
goods group shall contain “DropoutReport” basic object without attributes in the “attributes”
parameter for the goods group.
⎯ “Nicotine products”.
⎯ “Specialized food products and biologically active food additives”. A structure of the
request sent for specialized food products and biologically active food additives shall
contain the “DropoutReport” basic object without attributes in the “attributes” parameter for
the goods group.
⎯  “Antiseptic/antibacterial skin cleansers and hand sanitizers”. A structure of the request
sent for antiseptic/antibacterial skin cleansers and hand sanitizers shall contain the
“DropoutReport” basic object without attributes in the “attributes” parameter for the goods
group.
⎯ “Alternative tobacco products”.


---

Notes.

2) For “Tobacco products”, “Alternative tobacco products” and “Nicotine products” GGs, it is
permissible to indicate the shipping containers and group packings in the array of disposed MC
with indication of the attribute for writing-off of all contained elements
3) An array of disposed MCs of consumer and group packages for the “Tobacco products”,
“Alternative tobacco products” and “Nicotine products” GGs shall contain an identification code
(GTIN + Serial).

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.4.10. Method “Send Report on MC Aggregation”
This method is used to send report on MC aggregation to OMS and is used to aggregate
into the sets, group and shipping packages. Within the report, MC are sent without verification
code. In one document it is possible to form a maximum of 3 levels of aggregates, for example:
UNIT-> GROUP, GROUP -> SET, SET -> SSCC. New aggregation documents with
unitSerialNumber which were previously used for aggregation with three levels of nesting, will be
processed with error.  For the further aggregation of more than 3 levels of nesting it is
recommended to use the "Aggregation” document in "True_API" of the TT GIS. Description on
how to receive the security marker is provided in section 9
Note: currently, this method is available for the following goods groups:
- “Tobacco Products”
- “Dairy Products”
- “Alternative tobacco products”.
- “Packaged water”. A structure of the request sent for the packaged water shall contain the
“AggregationReport” basic object without extensions for the goods group.
- “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages”. A structure of the request sent for beer-
based beverages shall contain the “AggregationReport” basic object without extensions for the
goods group.
- “Nicotine products”.
- “Specialized food products and biologically active food additives”. A structure of the request
sent for specialized food products and biologically active food additives shall contain the
“AggregationReport” basic object without extensions for the goods group.
-  “Antiseptic/antibacterial skin cleansers and hand sanitizers”. A structure of the request
sent for antiseptic/antibacterial skin cleansers and hand sanitizers shall contain the
“AggregationReport” basic object without extensions for the goods group.
- “Medical products”. A structure of the request sent for medical products shall contain the
“AggregationReport” basic object without extensions for the goods group.
- “Juice products and non-alcoholic beverages”. A structure of the request sent for medical
products shall contain the “AggregationReport” basic object without extensions for the goods
group;
- “Radio-electronic products”. A structure of the request sent for medical products shall
contain the “AggregationReport” basic object without extensions for the goods group;
- “Optical fiber and fiber optic products”. A structure of the request sent for medical products
shall contain the “AggregationReport” basic object without extensions for the goods group;


---

## Page 188


188
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

- “Veterinary medicines”. A structure of the request sent for medical products shall contain
the “AggregationReport” basic object without extensions for the goods group.

4.4.10.1.
Request
Example of the REST request which contains “AggregationReport” basic object without
attributes in attributes parameter for the goods group, can be found in Figure 80.
POST /api/v3/aggregation?omsId=cdf12109-10d3-11e6-8b6f-0050569977a1 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json

The following specific features shall be taken into account when generating an aggregation
report for “Tobacco products” goods group:

1. “unitSerialNumber” (aggregation code) field.
This parameter may contain:
− shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier),
− group package identification code (blocks);
− shipping package code of the format determined by the circulation participant (from
34 to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : . < = > ? !).
2. “sntins” (goods code) field.
This parameter may contain:
− identification code of consumer package and group package (see section 8.1);
— shipping package code of the format determined by the circulation participant (from 34
to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : . < = > ? !).
3. Nesting logic.
− The group package identification codes (blocks) and shipping package codes of the
format determined by the goods circulation participant (from 34 to 74 characters long)
may be aggregated in the SSCC shipping package.


---

## Page 193


193
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

− Consumer package identification codes (packs) may be aggregated in the group
packing.
− The group package identification codes (blocks) and consumer package identification
codes (packs) may be aggregated in the shipping packages of the format determined
by the goods circulation participant (from 34 to 74 characters long).

Example of REST Request for Dairy Manufacturers (Partially Filled Aggregate)
Figure 83
A structure of the report on MC aggregation for dairy manufacturers contains
“AggregationReport” basic object without attributes in the “attributes” parameter for the goods
group, but it has specific features. If the aggregate is filled partially, a value to be sent in
“aggregationUnitCapacity” and “aggregatedItemsCount” fields is 1. Below are examples of REST
requests for dairy manufacturers (if the aggregate is filled partially or completely).
Notes:

1. “unitSerialNumber” (aggregation code) field.
This field may contain:
− shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier).
− shipping package code of the format determined by a circulation participant (from 18
to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : ; < = > ? !).
− group package identification code.
2. “sntins” (goods code) field:
This field may contain:
− identification code of consumer, group package (see section 8.8);
− shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier).
− shipping package code of the format determined by a circulation participant (from 18
to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : ; < = > ? !).
3. SSCC shipping packages, shipping packages of the format determined by a goods
circulation participant (from 18 to 74 characters long), identification codes of consumer
and group packages may be aggregated in the SSCC shipping package.


---

## Page 195


195
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.  The identification codes of consumer, group packages, the SSCC shipping packages,
shipping packages of the format determined by a participant (from 18 to 74 characters
long) may be aggregated in the shipping packages of the format determined by a
circulation participant (from 18 to 74 characters long).
5. Consumer package identification codes may be aggregated into the group package.

report for “Alternative tobacco products” goods group:

− shipping package code of the format determined by the goods circulation participant
(code of more than 34 characters containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet,
as well as special characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : . < = > ? !).
− shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier).
3. Nesting logic.

− The codes of SSCC shipping packages, codes of shipping packages of the format

## Page 198


198
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

The following specific features shall be taken into account when generating an aggregation
report for “Packaged water” goods group:

1. “unitSerialNumber” (aggregation code) field.
This parameter may contain:
− shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier);
− shipping package code of the format determined by the circulation participant (from
18 to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : ; < = > ? !);
− group package identification code.
2. “sntins” (goods code) field.
This parameter may contain:
− identification code of consumer and group package (see section 8.12);
− shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier);
− shipping package code of the format determined by the circulation participant (from
18 to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : ; < = > ? !).
3. Nesting logic.
− The SSCC shipping packages, shipping packages of the format determined by the
goods circulation participant (from 18 to 74 characters long), the identification codes of
consumer and group packages may be aggregated in the SSCC shipping package.
− The identification codes of consumer and group packages, the SSCC shipping
packages, shipping packages of the format determined by the participant (from 18 to
74 characters long) may be aggregated in the shipping packages of the format
determined by the goods circulation participant (from 18 to 74 characters long);
− Consumer package identification codes may be aggregated into the group package.

Example of REST request for “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages” goods
group
Figure 86
A structure of the report on MC aggregation for manufacturers of beer, beer-based and
low-alcohol beverages contains “AggregationReport” basic object without attributes in the
“attributes” parameter for goods group, yet has specific features:

1. “unitSerialNumber” (aggregation code) field.
This field may contain:
— Shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier).
— Shipping package code of the format determined by a circulation participant (from 18
to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : ; < = > ? !).
— Group package identification code (without verification code).
2. “sntins” (goods code) field.
This field may contain:
− identification code of consumer or group package (see section 8.13)
− shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier).


---

## Page 200


200
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

3. The following may be aggregated in the SSCC shipping package:
— Codes of SSCC shipping packages.
— Shipping package code of the format determined by a circulation participant (from 18
to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : ; < = > ? !).
— Identification codes of group and consumer packages.
4. The following may be aggregated into packages of the format determined by a
circulation participant (from 18 to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the
Latin alphabet, as well as special characters):
— Shipping packages of the format determined by a circulation participant (from 18 to 74
characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters) A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : ; < = > ? !.
— SSCC shipping packages.
— Identification codes of group and consumer packages.
5. Identification codes of the consumer packages, marking codes ordered for one goods
code (GTIN) may be aggregated into the group package.

The following specific features shall be taken into account when generating an aggregation
report for “Nicotine products” goods group:

1. “unitSerialNumber” (aggregation code) field.
This parameter may contain:
− shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier),
− identification code of the group package;
− shipping package code of the format determined by the goods circulation participant
(from 34 to 74 characters long), containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as
well as special characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : . < = > ? !).
2. “sntins” (goods code) field.
This parameter may contain:


---

## Page 202


202
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

− identification code of consumer package, group package (see section 8.14);
− shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier);
− shipping package code of the format determined by the goods circulation participant
(from 34 to 74 characters long), containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as
well as special characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : . < = > ? !).

3. Nesting logic
− The codes of SSCC shipping package, identification codes of group and consumer
packages or shipping package codes of the format determined by the goods
circulation participant (from 34 to 74 characters long) may be aggregated in the
SSCC shipping package.
− Consumer package identification codes may be aggregated into the group package.
− The identification codes of group and consumer packages or shipping package
codes of the format determined by the goods circulation participant (from 34 to 74
characters long) may be aggregated in the shipping packages of the format
determined by the goods circulation participant (from 34 to 74 characters long).
− When
aggregating
identification
codes
emitted
by
templateId=21
and
templateId=22 templates, IC in “sntins” (goods code) and “unitSerialNumber”
(aggregate code) parameters must contain a maximum retail sale price according
to the goods group template (a special divider ASCII 29 between a serial number
and a maximum retail sale price shall be excluded for identification codes emitted
by templateId=21).

may
contain:
— Shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier).
— Group package identification code.
— Shipping package code of the format determined by a circulation participant (from 18
to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : ; < = > ? !).
2. “sntins” (goods code) field may contain:
— Identification code of consumer package, group package (see subsection 8.15).
— Shipping package code (SSCC - length of 20 numeric symbols with GS1 AI 00
application identifier).
— Shipping package code of the format determined by a circulation participant (from 18
to 74 characters long containing digits and letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters A-Z a-z 0-9 % & ' " ( ) * + , - _ . / : ; < = > ? !).
3. The SSCC shipping packages, shipping packages of the format determined by a
circulation participant (from 18 to 74 characters long), identification codes of consumer,
group packages and sets may be aggregated in the SSCC shipping package.


---

204
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4. The identification codes of consumer and group packages, the SSCC shipping
packages, shipping packages of the format determined by a participant (from 18 to 74
characters long) may be aggregated in the shipping packages of the format determined
by a circulation participant (from 18 to 74 characters long);
5. Identification codes of the consumer packages, marking codes ordered for one goods
code (GTIN) may be aggregated into the group package.

4.4.10.2.
Response to Request
Example of JSON response can be found in Figure 95.
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Length: 74
Pragma: no-cache
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
Expires: 0
X-Frame-Options: DENY
X-Content--Options: nosniff
Content-Type: application/json;charset=UTF-8
Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, max-age=0, must-revalidate

{
"omsId":"CDF12109-10D3-11E6-8B6F-0050569977A1",
"reportId":"fab1c0e4-9590-4ed7-8d58-18862d6a9aab"
}

JSON Response Example
Figure 95
Upon successful completion of request, the server returns HTTP code -200 and a unique
identifier of report assigned by OMS. The obtained identifier of report on MC aggregation is used
to obtain report processing status (see cl. 4.4.13). For the structure of response to request to send
information on aggregation, see Table 126. For error codes, see subsection 6.2.
Table 126 – Format of Response to Request to Send Information on Aggregation
Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
omsId
Unique OMS identifier.
String value.
Identifier value in accordance with ISO/IEC
9834-8.
Template: [0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-
9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}
String
(36)
(UUID)
Yes
