4.4.11. Method “Send Report on MC Utilization (Application)”
This method is used to send a report on MC utilization to the OMS. Description on how
to receive the security marker is provided in section 9.

Notes:
1. This method is currently available to the participants for the following goods groups:
— “Tobacco products”;
— “Dairy products”;
— “Medicines for human use”;
— “Alternative tobacco products”;
— “Packaged water”;
— “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages”;
— “Nicotine products”;
— “Specialized food products and biologically active food additives”;
— “Antiseptic/antibacterial skin cleansers and hand sanitizers”;
—  “Non-alcoholic beer”;
— “Medical products”;
— “Juice products and non-alcoholic beverages”;
— “Seafood”;
— “Pet foods”;
— “Games and toys for children”;
— “Titanium metal products”;
— “Radio-electronic products”;
— “Optical fiber and fiber optic products”;
— “Vegetable oils”;
— “Cosmetics, household chemicals, and personal hygiene products”;
— “Canned foods”;


---

## Page 215


215
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

— “Veterinary medicines”;
— “Bicycles and bicycle frames”;
— “Building materials”;
— “Pyrotechnic articles and fire safety and firefighting equipment”;
— “Printed goods”;
— “Heaters”;
— “Groceries”;
— “Cabling and wiring products”;
— “Engine oils”;
— “Polymer pipes”;
— “Confectionery products”;
— “Auto parts and components for vehicles”;
— “Radio-electronic products. Electronic nicotine delivery systems”;
— “Radio-electronic products. Laptops and smartphones”;
— “Meat products”;
— “Feed additives”;
— “Home and kitchen goods”;
— “Industrial equipment”;
— “Fertilizers in consumer packaging”;
— “Semi-finished and frozen food products”;
— “Real fur”;
— “Pyrotechnic articles”.

2. The report is generated automatically for the following goods groups:
— “Light industry”
—  “Footwear”
—  “Perfume and eau de toilette”
—  “New pneumatic rubber tires and tire casings”
—  “Photo cameras (except cine cameras), flash lights and flash bulbs”
Following the results of sending of the report on the MC utilization to the OMS, it is
necessary to receive the report processing status (by using Method “Obtain the Report
Processing Status”). After having received the response, it is possible to use the “Receive receipt
by unique document identifier” method (see section 4.4.18 to receive a receipt).


---

216
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

toys for children” goods group), can be found in Figure 96.

X-Signature: {подпись}

    "0104604060002439215CQ9F6alYHJNc\u001d91FFD0\u001d92dGVzdLxK0Uy5CjmimCobix8MKu5C/EIMLSbpUoU0h2k=",
    "0104604060002439215nQXgxFY1VC8A\u001d91FFD0\u001d92dGVzdA+KZOgQ6TEX2miRwNAilbvzimN9Wc0NFcj4EaU="
  ]
}

attributes in the “attributes” parameter for the goods group (on the example of the
“Games and toys for children” goods group)

A description of the structure of “UtilisationReport” object used to send a report on MC
utilization to OMS can be found in Table 130.
Notes:
1) In parameters “sntins”, the sent marking codes shall include the full marking code with
verification code because this report is sent to emission registrar where marking code is
verified. The quantity of MC per report on utilization shall not exceed 30,000 codes.
2) For the “Dairy products” goods group, the actual weight of the product unit may also
be transferred in the marking code structure (templateId=20) (for more details, see
section 4.4.11.1.3).


---

## Page 218


218
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

3) For the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages” goods group, the actual volume
of the products may also be transferred (templateId=18) (for more details, see section
4.4.11.1.7).
4) For the “Non-alcoholic beer” goods group, the actual volume of the products may also
be transferred (templateId=28) (for more details, see section 4.4.11.1.9).
5) For the “Seafood” goods group, the actual weight of the product unit may also be
transferred in the marking code structure (templateId=27 and templateId=38) (for more
details, see section 4.4.11.1.13).
6) For the “Pet foods” goods group, the actual weight of the product unit may also be
transferred in the marking code structure (templateId=26 and templateId=41) (for more
details, see section 4.4.11.1.17).
7) For the “Medical products” goods group, a factory serial number of products may also
be transferred (templateId=12) (for more details, see section 4.4.11.1.10);
8) For the “Radio-electronic products” goods group, one of the variable characteristics
may also be transferred (templateId=36, templateId=37) (for more details, see section
4.4.11.1.15).
9) For the “Meat products” goods group, the actual weight of the product unit may also
be transferred in the marking code structure (templateId=32 and templateId=74) (for more
details, see section 4.4.11.1.36).
10) For the “Semi-finished and frozen food products” goods group, the actual weight of
the product unit may also be transferred in the marking code structure (templateId=83
and templateId=84) (for more details, see section 4.4.11.1.41).

Notes:
1. Method “Receiving the business places” may be used to receive information about business
places or production sites (kpp and fiasId) for the “Tobacco products” goods group.
2. In the report on MC utilization (application), the production date must be specified in the
“productionDate” attribute for units of goods. For group packages, the “productionDate”
attribute may be unfilled. The report is processed asynchronously. A processing result will be
in the result receipt.

## Page 224


224
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
and time. Date format: yyyy-
MM-ddTHH:mm:ss±hhmm
customsReceiverId
Identifier of goods location in
the customs control area
String (36)
(GUID)
No
See the note
below
Note: Value subjectId (value of subjectId):
1) for medicines produced in the territory of the Russian Federation: 14-character business
place identifier of party to circulation assigned upon the latter’s registration of business
place in MDLP FGIS;
2) for medicines produced outside the territory of the Russian Federation: 36-character
number assigned to the marketing authorization holder (or representative office thereof)
upon its registration in MDLP FGIS.
Attention!
1) For medicines produced in the territory of the Russian Federation:
− Field “orderType” is mandatory. This field shall contain a numerical value for
manufacturing order type: (1) for in-house and (2) for contract manufacturing;
− When orderType=2 is indicated, the value of field “ownerId” shall indicate 36-character
number assigned to the pharmaceutical entity which is the customer of contract
manufacturing upon its registration in MDLP FGIS;
− Fields “packingId”, “controlId” and “customsReceiverId” are left empty.
2) For medicines produced outside the territory of the Russian Federation:
− Field “packingId” is mandatory. It shall contain 36-character identifier assigned to
foreign counterparties upon their registration in MDLP FGIS by the medicine marketing
authorization holder (or by representative office thereof).
− Field “customsReceiverId” is non-mandatory. The field shall be filled in case
the medicines are marked in the customs control area. It shall contain an MDLP FGIS 36-
character identifier of goods location from the Registry of Places in the Customs-
Controlled Area.
− Field “controlId” is non-mandatory.
Attribute “controlId” becomes mandatory upon filling in of "customReceiverId" field, i.e.
the field shall be filled in if the medicines are marked in the customs control area.


---

## Page 225


225
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

The field “controlId” may be filled in for any medicines manufactured outside the Russian
Federation even if the marking is performed beyond the customs control area (in this case
the field shall be optional).
It shall contain a 36-character identifier assigned to foreign counterparties upon their
registration in MDLP FGIS by the medicine marketing authorization holder (or by its
representative office).
− Fields “orderType” and “ownerId” are left empty.

batchNumber

Note.
1. In the report on MC utilization (application) a product expiration date shall be
specified in the “expDate”, or “expDate72”, or “expDateConditions” attribute for
units of goods, and a production date can be specified in the “productionDate”
attribute. The “expDate”, or “expDate72”, or “expDateConditions”, and
“productionDate” attributes shall not be filled in for the group packages. The report
is processed asynchronously. The processing result will be in the result receipt.
2. When transferring a variable expiration date in the attribute “expDateConditions”,
all marking codes transferred in the report must contain the same GTIN. If marking
codes contain different goods codes, the report will not be accepted.

Indication of actual weight in the marking code structure (templateId=20) in the
application report
The actual weight of the unit of product can be indicated after mandatory data groups (after
verification code with symbol ASCII 29) in the marking code structure by using application
identifier AI = '3103'. In this way, the marking code structure (templateId=20) for the “Dairy
products” goods group in the application report will be as follows:


---

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Data
group
Length Format
AI
Type
1
14
Figures
01
Goods code according to the
corresponding goods nomenclature
(GTIN)
2
6
Symbol string
21
Good individual serial number
3
4
Symbol string
93
Verification code
4
6
Figures
3103
Actual weight (contains the weight
in kilograms), optional attribute.
Note: decimal point is assumed (but
not specified) after three digits for
fractional values.
Note:
1) one application report can contain marking codes with or without actual weight.
2) MCs with weight only for unit of goods (cisType = “UNIT”) may contain in the
report on utilization (application) for the “Dairy products” goods group.

103
0104640016748389215fp(IS\u001D93dGVz\u001D3103001324

Example of REST request for manufacturers of beer, beer-based and low-alcohol
beverages
Figure 107

For the marking codes of the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages” goods group
(“templateId” = “18”) which are specified in sntins parameter, information on volume (actual
volume, liters) can be transferred as well.
If information on a volume is not specified in the MC, the TT GIS can refuse an application
report: indication of the actual volume is mandatory for a marking code for a goods code with
“Keg” package characteristic.
An algorithm to indicate a volume in the MC is described below.
Example of the REST request for manufacturers of beer, beer-based beverages with
indication of a volume with AI = '3351', AI = '3353’, AI = '3350' in the MC structure and the MC
without volume can be found in Figure 108.

indication of a volume with AI = '3351'and AI = '3353’ in the MC structure
Figure 108
For the MCs specified in the example, a volume of the products will be 132,4 liters and
1,584 liters.
1. Example 1: A volume of 132,4 liters shall be written down as follows - AI 3351 001324;
2. Example 2: A volume of 1,584 liters shall be written down as follows - AI 3353 001584;
3. Example 3: A volume of 300 liters shall be written down as follows - AI 3350 000300.

beer-based and low-alcohol beverages can be found in Table 137.

## Page 238

Notes:
1. Method “Receiving the business places” can be used to receive information about
business places (kpp and fiasId) for the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol
beverages” goods group.
2. In the report on MC utilization (application) a product expiration date shall be specified
in the “expirationDate” attribute, and a production date shall be specified in the
“productionDate” attribute for the units of goods. The “expirationDate” and
“productionDate” attributes must not be filled in for the group packages. The report is
processed asynchronously. The processing result will be in the result receipt.
3. A report on MC utilization (application) shall be submitted to the system not later than
one calendar day from a day of manufacture of products.
4. The alcoholVolume parameter is mandatory for kegs, consumer packages, and group
packages. Validation of this requirement is performed asynchronously. The
processing result will be in a result receipt.
5. The documentNumber and documentDate parameters are mandatory for kegs,
consumer packages, and group packages containing goods manufactured in the
Russian Federation. Validation of these requirements is performed asynchronously.
The processing result will be in a result receipt.

Indication of actual volume in the marking code structure
A volume can be specified in the MC structure after mandatory data groups (after a
verification code with special divider having code 29 in ASCII character table) by using application
identifier AI = '3350' or AI = '3351' or AI = '3352' or AI = '3353' or AI = '3354' or AI = '3355'. In this
way, a marking code structure for the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages” goods group
(templateId=18) in the application report will be as follows:
Data
group
Length
Format
AI
Type
1
14
Figures
01
Goods code according to the
corresponding goods
nomenclature (GTIN)
2
7 or 13
Symbol string 21
Good individual serial number
3
4
Symbol string 93
Verification code
4
6
Figures
335Y, where Y
can have values
from 0 to 5
Volume (contains volume in
liters), optional attribute.
Notes:
1. One application report can contain marking codes with or without volume.


---

## Page 239


239
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

2. One report can contain MCs with volume which have different application identifiers:
AI = '3350' or AI = '3351' or AI = '3352' or AI = '3353' or AI = '3354' or AI = '3355';
3. Y value (from 0 to 5) in AI application identifier indicates the number of decimal places
in a value of the actual volume.
Example of the MC (templateId=18) with optional volume (with serial number of 7
characters) and application identifier AI = '3353’ can be found in Figure 109.
0104640016748389215ffffff\u001D93dGVz\u001D3353001324

(01)04640016748389 - Goods code
(21)5ffffff\u001D - Individual serial number of the item with a country code at the beginning
+ symbol ASCII 29
(93)dGVz\u001D - Verification code + symbol ASCII 29
(3353)001324 - actual volume (in this case 1 l 324 ml)
Example of the MC (templateId=18) with optional volume (with serial number of 7
characters) and application identifier AI = '3353'

{

    "0123859334576487215ew3212\u001d93dGVz\u001e0123859334576487215nm3212\u001d93dGVz",

Example of the REST request for manufacturers of beer, beer-based and low-alcohol
beverages for a misgrading operation
Figure 112
Sending an application report for a misgrading operation utilisationType = RESORT is
available for the goods group. To generate this type of the report, the following conditions shall
be met:
1. The report shall contain the "participantId” mandatory attribute and "kpp" (for legal
entities) or "fiasId" (for physical entities) conditionally mandatory attributes.
2. The report shall not contain the "productionDate", "expirationDate", "alcoholVolume",
"documentNumber" and "documentDate” attributes.
3. The report shall contain unique marking codes. The marking codes in the "sntins” string
array that correspond to the following format:
— A marking code shall contain the main code and misgrading code separated by ASCII
code 30. The main marking code and misgrading marking code shall contain the full
marking code, including a verification code, and shall conform to a goods group template;
— The main marking code and misgrading marking code shall have the same goods
code;
— The marking code and misgrading marking code shall not contain information about
volume of goods.
An example of the marking codes in the application report for a misgrading operation for
the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages” goods group can be found in Figure 113.
0104640002480019215АААААА\u001d93dGVz\u001e0104640002480019215NNNNNN\u001d93dGVz

Notes:

2. In the report on MC utilization (application) a product expiration date shall be specified

“productionDate” attributes may not be filled in for the group packages. The report is
processed asynchronously. The processing result will be in the result receipt.

information system must be specified in the report on utilization (application) of MCs (raw
materials of GTIN of which are subject to traceability in VetIS). The exception is when a
method of introduction into circulation is “Remarking”. In this case the “vetisGuid” attribute
may be unfilled.

Figure 120
Information on a factory serial number can be transferred as well in the marking codes
(which are specified in the sntins parameter) of the “Medical products” goods group
(templateId=12).
An application report must contain information on batch No. in the “seriesNumber” attribute
or shipment No. in the “batchNumber” attribute, or a factory serial number in a marking code
structure which is specified in the fifth group for all marking codes.

(01) 4650117240163- Goods code
(21)5100000005NgF\u001D - Individual serial number of the item with a country code at the beginning +

AI = '97'
Figure 121
Marking codes uniqueness must be ensured by a marking code without taking into
account a factory serial number, otherwise the report will be rejected.
Uniqueness of a factory serial number must be ensured within one goods item (gtin).

volume with AI = '97' in the MC structure can be found in Figure 122.

in the application report will be as follows:

## Page 250

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

1. In the report on MC utilization (application) a production date shall be specified in the
“productionDate” attribute, and a product expiration date may be specified in the
“expirationDate” attribute for the units of goods if the “Expiration date” parameter is
specified in a goods card. The “expirationDate” and “productionDate” attributes may not
be filled in for the group packages and sets. The report is processed asynchronously. The
processing result will be in the result receipt.
2. The report must contain information on batch No. in the “seriesNumber” attribute or
shipment No. in the “batchNumber” attribute, or a factory serial number in a marking code
structure which is specified in the fifth group for all marking codes.

Indication of actual weight in the marking code structure (templateId=27 or
templateId=38) in the application report.
Actual weight of the unit of product can be indicated after mandatory data groups (after
verification code with symbol ASCII 29) in the marking code structure by using application
identifier AI = '3103'. In this way, a marking code structure for the “Seafood” goods group
(templateId=27) in the application report will be as follows:

Actual weight (contains weight in kilograms),
optional attribute. Note: decimal point is

for fractional values.

Marking code structure for the “Seafood” goods group (templateId=38) in the application
report will be as follows:
Code

4

3103
Actual weight (contains weight in kilograms),
optional attribute. Note: decimal point is
assumed (but not specified) after three digits
for fractional values.

Note:
1. One application report can contain marking codes with or without actual weight.
2. One application report can contain marking codes emitted both through templateId=27
and templateId=38
3. An application report can contain marking codes with an actual weight only for the units
of goods and an attribute of variable weight is specified in the goods card.
Example of the marking code (templateId=27) with the actual weight can be found in Figure
127.

Example of REST request for manufacturers of radio-electronic products
Figure 130
A variable characteristic can be transferred as well in the marking codes (which are
specified in the sntins parameter) of the “Radio-electronic products” goods group (templateId=36,
templateId=37). The variable characteristic can contain one of the following:
1. Quantity of goods - to indicate goods quantity, (AI30) is supposed to be used:
— unit of measurement: quantity (pcs);
— minimum value: 1 - (example: 30 1);
— maximum value: 99 999 999 - (example: 30 99999999);
— regular expression: (\d{1,8});
— AI length: variable length from 1 to 8;
2. Weight of goods – to indicate weight of goods, AI310Y (3100-3105) is supposed to be
used:
— unit of measurement: kilogram (kg);
— minimum value: 0.00001 - (example: 3105 000001);
— maximum value: 999 999 - (example: 3100 999999);
— regular expression: (\d{6});
— AI range: 3100-3105;
— AI length: fixed;
— note*: the fourth digit (0-5) of this AI specifies the number of decimal places.
3. Length of goods – to indicate length of goods, AI311Y (3110-3115) is supposed to be
used:
— measurement unit: meter (m);


---

## Page 259


259
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

— minimum value: 0.00001 - (example: 3115 000001);
— maximum value: 999 999 - (example: 3110 999999);
— regular expression: (\d{6});
— AI range: 3110-3115;
— AI length: fixed;
— note*: the fourth digit (0-5) of this AI specifies the number of decimal places.
4. Volume of goods – to indicate volume of goods, AI335Y (3350 - 3355) is supposed to
be used:
— unit of measurement: liters (l);
— minimum value: 0.00001 - (example: 3355 000001);
— maximum value: 999 999 - (example: 3350 999999);
— regular expression: (\d{6});
— AI range: 3350-3355;
— AI length: fixed;
— note*: the fourth digit (0-5) of this AI specifies the number of decimal places.
An algorithm to indicate a variable characteristic in the MC is described below.
An example of the REST request for manufacturers of radio-electronic products with
indication of volume with AI = '30', AI = '3103', AI = '3113', AI = '3352’ in the MC structure and an
MC without volume can be found in Figure 131.

Content-Type: application/json
X-Signature: {подпись}

  "sntins": [
    "0104008638435016215OOOOOOOOOOOO1111111\u001d93dGVz",
    "0104008638435016215NNNNNNNNNNNN2222222\u001d93dGVz\u001D302",
    "0104008638435016215MMMMMMMMMMMM3333333\u001d93dGVz\u001D3103001584",
    "0104008638435016215YYYYYYYYYYYY4444444\u001d93dGVz\u001D3113020000",
    "0104008638435016215QQQQQQQQQQQQ5555555\u001d93dGVz\u001D3352001584"

1. Example 1: Goods quantity (2 pieces) shall be written down as follows - AI 30 2;

3103 001584;
3. Example 3: Length of goods of 20 meters shall be written down as follows - AI 3113
020000;
4. Example 3: Volume of goods of 15.84 liters shall be written down as follows - AI 3352
001584.

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

2. Method “Receiving the business places” can be used to obtain information about
business places (kpp and fiasId) for the “Radio-electronic products” goods group.
Submission of the application report for the operation of marking of goods from
the composition of disaggregated set
An example of a REST request for manufacturers of radio-electronic products for the
operation of marking of goods from the composition of disaggregated set is shown in Figure 132.
POST /api/v3/utilisation?omsId=cdf12109-10d3-11e6-8b6f-0050569977a1 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Content-Type: application/json
X-Signature: {подпись}

{
  "productGroup": "radio",
  "utilisationType": "SPLITMARK",
  "sntins": [
    "0104008638435016215OOOOOOOOOOOO1111111\u001d93dGVz\u001e0104008638435016215NNNNNNNNNNNN2222222"
,
    "0104008638435016215MMMMMMMMMMMM3333333\u001d93dGVz\u001e0104008638435016215YYYYYYYYYYYY4444444"
  ],
  "attributes": {
    "participantId": "1123456789"
  }
}
Example of the REST request for manufacturers of radio-electronic products for the
operation of marking of goods from the composition of disaggregated set
Figure 132
For this goods group, submission of an application report for the operation of marking of
goods from the composition of disaggregated set utilisationType = SPLITMARK is available. To
generate this type of the report, the following conditions must be met:
1. The report must contain a conditionally mandatory attribute "participantId".
2. The report must not contain the "productionDate”, "kpp" and "fiasId" attributes.
3. The report must contain unique marking codes in the "sntins” string array, formatted as
follows:
— A marking code must contain the main marking code and identification code, separated
by ASCII code 30 character;
— Both the main marking code and the identification code must comply with the goods
group template; the main marking code must include the verification code; the
identification code must be provided without a verification code (GTIN+SERIAL);
— The main marking code and the identification code must have the same goods code.

from the composition of disaggregated set for the “Radio-electronic products” goods group is
shown in Figure 133.

disaggregated set
Figure 133
Submission of the application report for goods separation operation
An example of the REST request for manufacturers of radio-electronic products for the
goods separation operation can be found in Figure 134.

Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Content-Type: application/json
X-Signature: {подпись}

{

  "utilisationType": "DIVISIONMARK",
  "sntins": [
    "0104008638435016215OOOOOOOOOOOO1111111\u001d93dGVz\u001D3103001584\u001e0104008638435016215NNNN
NNNNNNNN2222222\u001d93dGVz",
    "0104008638435016215MMMMMMMMMMMM3333333\u001d93dGVz\u001D302\u001e0104008638435016215YYYYYYYYYYY
Y4444444\u001d93dGVz"

Example of the REST request for manufacturers of radio-electronic products for the
operation of separation of goods
Figure 134
Sending an application report for the goods separation operation utilisationType =
DIVISIONMARK is available for the goods group. To generate this report type, the following
conditions must be met:
1. The report must include the conditionally mandatory attribute "participantId".
2. The report must not contain the “productionDate”, “kpp”, and "fiasId” attributes.
3. The report must include unique marking codes in the "sntins” string array that
correspond to the following format:
0104008638435016215OOOOOOOOOOOO1111111\u001d93dGVz\u001e0104008638435016215NNNNNNNNNNNN2222222

— Each marking code must contain the main marking code and the marking code of the
original item, separated by the ASCII code 30. Both codes must comply with the goods
group template, including the verification code;
— The main marking code must correspond to the goods group template, include a
verification code, and be indicated with the following application identifiers that determine
a variable characteristic value:  'AI310Y ' or  'AI311Y ' or  'AI335Y ' or 'AI30';
— The main marking code and the marking code of the original item must have the same
goods code.

Indication of actual weight in the marking code structure (templateId=26 or
templateId=41) in the application report.
The actual weight of the product unit can be indicated in a marking code structure after
mandatory data groups (after a verification code with symbol ASCII 29) by using application
identifier AI = '3103'. In this way, a marking code structure for the “Pet foods” goods group
(templateId=26) in the application report will be as follows:
Code
Length
Format
AI
Type
1
14
Figures
01
Goods code according to the corresponding
goods nomenclature (GTIN)
2
6
Symbol string
21
Goods individual serial number
3
4
Symbol string
91
Verification key
4
44
Symbol string
92
Verification code
5
6
Figures
3103
Actual weight (contains weight in kilograms),
optional data group.
Note: decimal point is assumed (but not
specified) after three digits for fractional
values.

A marking code structure for the “Pet foods” goods group (templateId=41) in the application
report will be as follows:
Code
Length
Format
AI
Type
1
14
Figures
01
Goods code according to the corresponding
goods nomenclature (GTIN)
2
6
Symbol string
21
Goods individual serial number
3
4
Symbol string
93
Verification code
4
6
Figures
3103
Actual weight (contains weight in kilograms),
optional data group.
Note: decimal point is assumed (but not
specified) after three digits for fractional
values.

Note:
1. One application report can contain marking codes with or without actual weight.
2. One application report can contain marking codes emitted both through templateId=26
and templateId=41.
3. An application report can contain marking codes with an actual weight only for the units
of goods and an attribute of variable weight is specified in a goods card.
An example of the marking code (templateId=26) with the actual weight can be found in
Figure 139.

exceeding 72 hours) with indication of
storage condition index. A set of
values of expiration dates shall be

Notes:
1. In the report on MC utilization (application) a product expiration date shall be specified
in the “expirationDate” or “expDateConditions” attribute, and a production date shall be
specified in the “productionDate” attribute for the units of goods. These attributes may not
be filled in for group packages and sets. The report is processed asynchronously. The
processing result will be in the result receipt.
2. When transferring a variable expiration date in the attribute “expDateConditions”, all
marking codes transferred in the report must contain the same GTIN. If marking codes
contain different goods codes, the report will not be accepted.

Notes:
1. In the report on MC utilization (application) a product expiration date shall be
specified for units of goods in the “expirationDate” attribute if in a goods card in the
NCMG the “serviceLifeCharacteristic” attribute (“Characteristic of period of use of
the goods”) is unfilled or the “Expiration date” parameter has been specified, a
production date shall be specified in the “productionDate” attribute and information
about actual ethyl alcohol content (volume share, in percentages) shall be specified
in the “alcoholVolume” attribute. The “expirationDate”, “productionDate” and
“alcoholVolume” attributes may be unfilled for the group packages and sets. The
report is processed asynchronously. A processing result will be in a result receipt.
2. Information about an actual ethyl alcohol content is transferred as a whole number
or decimal number (with rounding to 1 decimal place). If there is no ethyl alcohol in
the composition, it is necessary to indicate 0 as a value.

attribute, and a batch number in the “seriesNumber” attribute for the units of goods. The
“expirationDate”, “productionDate” and “seriesNumber” attributes may not be filled in for the group
packages. The report is processed asynchronously. The processing result will be in the result
receipt.

Submission of the application report for the operation of marking of goods from
the composition of disaggregated set
An example of the REST request for manufacturers of games and toys for children for the
operation of marking of goods from the composition of disaggregated set can be found in Figure
149.
POST /api/v3/utilisation?omsId=cdf12109-10d3-11e6-8b6f-0050569977a1 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Content-Type: application/json
X-Signature: {подпись}

{
  "productGroup": "toys",
  "utilisationType": "SPLITMARK",
  "sntins": [
    "010465011724016321505NgFUUIUY12\u001d910024\u001d92UjpfAmE6elcVxvtPJHHGl/8ULzQB8YPU/XnOERdyuQ==
\u001e 0104650117240163215NNNNNNNNNNNN",
    "010465011724016321545NgF5678wrt\u001d910024\u001d92UjpfAmE6elcVxvtPJHHGl/8ULzQB8YPU/YUOEpdyuQ==
\u001e0104650117240163215YYYYYYYYYYYY"
  ],
  "attributes": {
    "participantId": "1123456789"
  }
}
Example of the REST request for manufacturers of games and toys for children for
operation of marking of goods from the composition of disaggregated set
Figure 149
For this goods group, submission of the application report for the operation of marking of
goods from the composition of disaggregated set utilisationType = SPLITMARK is available. To
generate this type of the report, the following conditions shall be met:
1. The report must contain a conditionally mandatory attribute "participantId".
2. The report must not contain the "productionDate” and "expirationDate" attributes.
3. The report must contain unique marking codes in the "sntins” string array that
correspond to the following format:
— The marking code must contain the main marking code and the identification code,
separated by ASCII code 30 character;
— Both the main marking code and the identification code must correspond to a goods
group template; the main marking code must include the verification code; the
identification code must be indicated without a verification code (GTIN+SERIAL);


---

## Page 279


279
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

— The main marking code and the identification code must have the same goods code.
An example of the marking codes in the application report for operation of marking of goods
from the composition of disaggregated set for the “Games and toys for children” goods group is
shown in Figure 150.
Example of the marking codes for operations of marking of goods from the composition
of disaggregated set
Figure 150

attribute) and production date (the “productionDate” attribute) are specified for units of
goods. These attributes must be unfilled for group packages and for goods related to

processed asynchronously. A processing result will be in a result receipt.

expDateConditions

Product expiration date (for a shelf
life exceeding 72 hours) with
indication of a storage condition
index. A set of expiration dates
values shall be specified in this
attribute. Each nesting of the set
shall include the storage condition
index specified in the NCMG and
expiration date (greater or equal to
the current date) in a format of yyyy-
MM-dd. Indication of the several
expiration dates is allowed. A storage
condition index in a set shall be
unique within a value set.
Object
(Json Object)
No
See the note
below

Notes:
1. In the report on MC utilization (application) a product expiration date shall be
specified in the “expirationDate” or “expDateConditions” attribute, and a production
date shall be specified in the “productionDate” attribute for the units of goods. The
“expirationDate”, “expDateConditions” and “productionDate” attributes may be
unfilled for the sets. The report is processed asynchronously. A processing result
will be in a result receipt.
2. When transferring a variable expiration date in the “expDateConditions” attribute, all
marking codes transferred in the report must contain the same GTIN. If marking
codes contain different goods codes, the report will not be accepted.

expDateConditions

Product expiration date (shelf life
exceeding 72 hours) with a storage
condition index. A set of expiration
date values shall be specified in this
attribute. Each nested item in the set
shall include the storage condition
index specified in the NCMG and an
expiration date (greater than or equal
to the current date) in yyyy-MM-dd
format. Several expiration dates may
be specified. The storage condition
index in a set shall be unique within a

below

Notes:
1. In the report on MC utilization (application) a product expiration date shall be specified in the
“expirationDate” or “expDateConditions” attribute, and a production date shall be specified in
the “productionDate” attribute for the units of goods. The “expirationDate” and
“productionDate” attributes may be unfilled for the group packages and sets. The report is
processed asynchronously. A processing result will be in a result receipt.
2. When transferring the variable expiration date in the “expDateConditions” attribute, all marking
codes transferred in the report must contain the same GTIN. If marking codes contain different
goods codes, the report will be rejected.

## Page 299


299
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Indication of the actual weight in the marking code structure (templateId=32 or
templateId=74) in the application report.
The actual weight of the product unit can be specified after mandatory data groups (after a
verification code with symbol ASCII 29) in the marking code structure by using application
identifier AI = '3103'. In this way, the marking code structure for the “Meat products” goods group
(templateId=74) in the application report will be as follows:
Code
Length Format
AI
Type
1
14
Figures
01
Goods code according to the corresponding
goods nomenclature (GTIN)
2
13
Symbol string
21
Goods individual serial number
3
4
Symbol string
91
Verification key
4
44
Symbol string
92
Verification code
5
6
Figures
3103
Actual weight (contains weight in kilograms),
optional attribute.
Note: decimal point is assumed (but not
specified) after three digits for fractional values.

The marking code structure for the “Meat products” goods group (templateId=32) in the
application report will be as follows:
Code
Length Format
AI
Type
1
14
Figures
01
Goods code according to the corresponding goods
nomenclature (GTIN)
2
6
Symbol
string
21
Goods individual serial number
3
4
Symbol
string
93
Verification code
4
6
Figures
3103
Actual weight (contains weight in kilograms), optional
attribute.
Note: decimal point is assumed (but not specified)
after three digits for fractional values.

Note:
1. One application report can contain marking codes with or without actual weight.
2. One application report can contain marking codes emitted both through templateId=32
and templateId=74.
3. The application report can contain marking codes with the actual weight indicated only
for the units of goods and only when an attribute of variable weight is specified in a goods
card.
An example of the marking code (templateId=74) with the actual weight can be found in
Figure 169.

Notes:
1. In the report on MC utilization (application) a product expiration date shall be specified in the
“expirationDate” attribute, and a production date shall be specified in the “productionDate”
attribute for the units of goods. The “expirationDate” and “productionDate” attributes may be
unfilled for the group packages and sets. The report is processed asynchronously. A
processing result will be in a result receipt.
2. When transferring the variable expiration date in the “expDateConditions” attribute, all marking
codes transferred in the report must contain the same GTIN. If marking codes contain different
goods codes, the report will be rejected.

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Indication of actual weight in the marking code structure (templateId=83 or templateId=84)
in the application report.
The actual weight of the product unit may be indicated in the marking code structure after
the mandatory data groups (after the verification code with symbol ASCII 29), using application
identifier AI = '3103'. Accordingly, the marking code structure for the “Semi-finished and frozen
food products” goods group (templateId=84) in the application report will be as follows:
Code
Length
Format
AI
Type
1
14
Figures
01
Goods code according to the corresponding
goods nomenclature (GTIN)
2
13
Symbol string
21
Goods individual serial number
3
4
Symbol string
91
Verification key
4
44
Symbol string
92
Verification code
5
6
Figures
3103
Actual weight (in kilograms), optional attribute.
Note: the decimal point is assumed (but not
specified) after the third digit for fractional values.

The marking code structure for the “Semi-finished and frozen food products” goods group
(templateId=83) in the application report will be as follows:
Code
Length
Format
AI
Type
1
14
Figures
01
Goods code according to the corresponding
goods nomenclature (GTIN)
2
6
Symbol string
21
Goods individual serial number
3
4
Symbol string
93
Verification code
4
6
Figures
3103
Actual weight (in kilograms), optional attribute.
Note: the decimal point is assumed (but not
specified) after the third digit for fractional values.

Notes:
1. A single application report may contain marking codes both with and without actual
weight.
2. A single application report may contain marking codes issued under both
templateId=83 and templateId=84.
3. The application report can contain marking codes with the actual weight only for the
units of goods, and an attribute of variable weight is specified in the goods card.
An example of the marking code (templateId=84) with the actual weight can be found in
Figure 178.

## Page 313

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4.4.12. Method “Send a Notification of Acceptance of ICs for Record-keeping Purposes”
This method is used to send a notification of acceptance of the identification codes (that
were previously withdrawn from circulation with the “misdescription of identification codes”
reason) for record-keeping purposes. A description on how to receive a security marker is
provided in section 9.
Note: this method is currently available to participants for the following goods groups:
— “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages”.
Following the results of sending the notification of acceptance of ICs for record-keeping
purposes to the OMS, it is necessary to get a report processing status (by using the Method
“Obtain the Report Processing Status”). After having received a response, it becomes possible to
use the “Receive receipt by unique document identifier” method (See subsection 4.4.18 to get a
receipt).
4.4.12.1.
Request
An example of the REST request with the “SurplusReport” object for the “Beer, beer-
based and low-alcohol beverages” goods group can be found in Figure 183.
POST /api/v3/surplus?omsId=cdf12109-10d3-11e6-8b6f-0050569977a1 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Content-Type: application/json
X-Signature: {подпись}

{
  "documentType": "SURPLUS_POSTING",
  "documentVersion": "1.0",
  "documentDate": "2022-03-11T05:10:00.872791Z",
  "participantInn": "7825706086",
  "primaryDocumentCustomName": "Возврат в оборот",
  "primaryDocumentDate": "2022-03-11",
  "primaryDocumentNumber": "81543b17-fdab-439a-9029-45d911729358",
  "codes": [
    "000000462095287,b4*i%93dGVz",
    "00000046200310U?:HhVK93dGVz"
  ]
}

An example of the REST request with the “UtilisationReport” object for the “Beer, beer-
based and low-alcohol beverages” goods group
Figure 183
A structure of the JSON request to send a notification of acceptance of ICs for record-
keeping purposes to the OMS.

## Page 315


315
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Note: marking codes sent as “codes” parameters shall be in a format of the full marking
code with a verification code because this report is sent to an emission registrar where a marking
code is verified. Marking codes shall not contain additional optional application identifiers.
Quantity of the MCs in the notification of acceptance of ICs for record-keeping purposes shall not
exceed 30,000 codes.

4.4.13. Method “Obtain the Report Processing Status”
This method is applied to obtain the report processing status by using the following
parameters: security marker and OMS identifier; report identifier. Description on how to receive
the security marker is provided in section 9.
Note: to find out the details of the report processing, it is necessary to obtain a receipt using
the method “Receive a receipt by unique document identifier” (see Section 4.4.18).

4.4.13.1.
Request
REST request example is shown in Figure 185.

0050569977a1 HTTP/1.1

clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f

4.4.14. Method “Getting a list of Service Providers”
This method is used to get a list of service providers serving the GCP by using the following
parameters: security marker (token), OMS identifier.  Description on how to receive the security
marker is provided in section 9
4.4.14.1.
Request
Example of request is shown in Figure 187.

4.4.15. Method “Obtaining the list of identifiers of the reports “Application data””
This method is used to obtain the list of identifiers of the “Application data” reports
received from the service provider.  Security marker (token) is sent to the server within HTTP-
header with “clientToken” name (it can be transferred in the “Authorization” parameter for the
“Medicines for human use” goods group).
Description on how to receive a security marker is provided in section 9.

4.4.15.1.
Request
Example of request is shown in Figure 189.
GET/api/v3/quality?orderId=fab1c0e4-9590-4ed7-8d58-18862d6a9aab&omsId=d1bc8149-7b39-4aa2-afb1-
df1b6c8f80c5&limit=10&skip=1 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f

4.4.16. Method “Obtaining the list of ICs from the “Application data” report”
This method is used to obtain the list of ICs (MC without verification code) with the printing
quality class specified in the report. The report ID obtained by means of the method “Obtaining
the list of identifiers of the reports “Application data”” shall be specified as “reportId” parameter
(Section 4.4.15). Security marker (token) is sent to the server within HTTP-header with the
“clientToken” name (it can be transferred in the “Authorization” parameter for the “Medicines for
human use” goods group).
Description on how to receive a security marker is provided in section 9.

4.4.16.1.
Request
Example of request is shown in Figure 192.
GET/api/v3/quality/cisList?reportId=fab1c0e4-9590-4ed7-8d58-18862d6a9a67&omsId=d1bc8149-7b39-4aa2-
afb1-df1b6c8f80c5 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f

4.4.17. Method “Receiving the business places”
This method is used to obtain information about business places and is available for the
following goods groups:
“Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages”;
“Non-alcoholic beer”;
“Specialized food products and biologically active food additives”;
“Juice products and non-alcoholic beverages”;
“Tobacco products”;
“Alternative tobacco products”;


---

## Page 333


333
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

“Nicotine products”;
“Radio-electronic products”;
“Radio-electronic products. Laptops and smartphones”;
“Building materials”.

Request
Example of REST request is shown in Figure 194.
GET/api/v3/mod?omsId=d1bc8149-7b39-4aa2-afb1-df1b6c8f80c5 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Host: localhost:8080

4.4.18. Method “Receive a receipt by unique document identifier”
This method is used to obtain receipts by unique identifier of the document (order, report
or MC block) as part of Receipting 2.0. This method is used the following parameters: security
marker (token), OMS identifier, identifier of the document (order, report or MC block).
Note: receipts formed during Receipting 1.0 cannot be received by using this method.

4.4.18.1.
Request
Example of request is shown in Figure 197.
GET/api/v3/receipts/receipt?omsId=53ecde9e-a686-4934-a337-ae319c944242&resultDocId=05f52b01-ba4b-
4dc7-a94a-7846db44ac63 HTTP/1.1
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Accept: application/json;

4.4.19. Method “Obtaining receipt by set filters”
This method is used to obtain receipts by set filters within Receipting 2.0. This method
uses the following parameters: security marker (token), OMS identifier, filters, which will be used
for search.
4.4.19.1.
Restrictions
Receipts, which were created during Receipting 1.0 cannot be obtained using this method.
Total amount of the returned data is limited to 100 records.
A product of the values of the skip and limit parameters should not exceed 10000 (inclusive).

4.4.19.2.
Request
Example of request is shown in Figure 199.
POST/api/v3/receipts/receipt/search?omsId=53ecde9e-a686-4934-a337-ae319c944242
HTTP/1.1
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "filter": {
    "startCreateDocDate": 1632480396000,
    "endCreateDocDate": 1632480396000,
    "startStartDocDate": 1632480396000,
    "endStartDocDate": 1632480397000,
    "resultDocIds": ["f49075a7-e997-4642-89da-6b6facc15f0a", "f49077a7-e997-4642-89da-6b6facc15f0a"],
    "sourceDocIds": ["f49075a7-e997-4642-89da-6b6facc15f0a"],
    "orderIds": ["f49077a7-e997-4642-89da-6b6facc15f0a"],
    "serviceProviderIds": ["f49075a7-e997-4642-89da-6b6facc15f0a"],
    "resultCodes": [0, 1, 4],
    "productGroups": ["milk"],
    "workflowTypes": ["REPORT_UTILIZE"],
    "productionOrderIds": ["af5fb948-d0c5-4e52-96d6-6af442798085"]
  },

Upon successful request completion, the server returns HTTP code 200 and array of the
receipts corresponding to the filters specified. A structure of the response to request is given in
Table 212. If receipts have not been found, an empty array of objects will be returned.
In case of error, HTTP code 400 will be returned with indication of the error and if service
is unavailable, HTTP code 500 will be returned.

4.4.20. Method “Receiving a document related to receipt”
This method is used to obtain a document related to a receipt. These documents are:
1. Source document (document sent by a participant). Identifier of the document related to
the receipt (docId) in this case is equal to a value of docId parameter in the first event of
the receipt (event structure can be found in Table 378).
2. Document with marking codes that were processed with error when routing to processing:
a. if a participant submits an application report;
b. if a participant submits a rejection report (for all goods groups, except "Alternative
tobacco products", "Nicotine products" and "Tobacco products”);
c. if automatic cancellation report is formed in the OMS (it is formed when closing order
with marking codes with “Active” status or if MCs in the order are expired);
d. if automatic application report is formed when MCs are issued.
In this case, identifier of the document (docId) related to the receipt is docId
parameter of the event “Dispatch of report for processing” (event structure can be found in
Table 378). If all marking codes have been sent successfully for processing during the
report processing, docId parameter will be missing in this event.
3. Document with marking code processing results in the processing:
a. when a participant submits an application report;
b. when a participant submits a rejection report;
c. when a participant submits an aggregation report;
d. when automatic cancellation report is formed in the OMS (it is formed when closing
order with marking codes with “Active” status or if MCs in the order are expired);
e. when automatic application report is formed when MCs are issued.


---

## Page 347


347
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

In this case, identifier of the document (docId) related to the receipt is docId
parameter of the event “Report processing result” (event structure can be found in Table
378). For the rejection report (for the “Tobacco products”, “Nicotine products”, “Alternative
tobacco products” goods groups) and for the aggregation report, this document will be
formed only for those marking codes, which have been processed with error (if MCs have
been processed without error, docId parameter will be missing in this event). For the
remaining reports, the document will be formed for all marking codes regardless of their
processing result.

A security marker (token) is sent to the server in HTTP-header with the “clientToken”
name (it can be transferred in the “Authorization” parameter for the “Medicines for human use”
goods group).

4.4.20.1.
Request
Example of request is shown in Figure 202.
GET /api/v3/receipts/document?resultDocId=15499583-9aa7-429a-bc33-335f0f4b0f93&docId=b024ae09-ef7c-
449e-b461-05d8eb116c79&omsId=7ce310f-5102-4057-9745-69d8d4b98ba4 HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f
Request Example
Figure 202

4.4.21. Method “Search of documents”
This method is used to search for the GCP’s documents (awaiting sending to the system)
with the following statuses:
1.
Order with CREATED status;
2.
Application report with DRAFT or PENDING status;
3.
Aggregation report with PENDING status;
4.
Rejection report with PENDING status;
5.
Notification of acceptance of ICs for record-keeping purposes with PENDING status.

Note: to view the document, it is necessary to use the method “Get document contents”
(see
Section
4.4.22)

4.4.21.1.
Request
Example of request is shown in Figure 205.


---

351
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f

4.4.22. Method “Get document contents”
This method is intended to receive a content of the GCP’s document by identifier (docId),
which has been received by means of the “Search of documents” method (see Section 4.4.21).
Note: after a content of the document has been received by the GCP, the document can
be deleted by means of the “Delete document” method (see Section 4.4.23) or can be signed by
means of the “Sign document” method (see Section 4.4.24).
4.4.22.1.
Request
Example of request is shown in Figure 208.
GET/api/v3/documents/content?omsId=cdf12109-10d3-11e6-8b6f-0050569977a1&docId=04990484-55f3-4289-96f0-
ca8bdd2c9e8d HTTP/1.1

clientToken: 1cecc8fb-fb47-4c8a-af3d-d34c1ead8c4f

4.4.23. Method “Delete document”
This method is intended to delete a GCP’s document with the following statuses:
1.
Order with CREATED status;
2.
Application report with DRAFT or PENDING status;
3.
Aggregation report with PENDING status;
4.
Rejection report with PENDING status;
5.
Notification of acceptance of ICs for record-keeping purposes with PENDING status.

4.4.26. Method “Provision of the list of installations of the integration solution (external
connection)”
This method is used to receive a GCP’s list of the installations of the integration solution
(external connection). In this method, <url of environment> means a basic address of the
environment that hosts API to work with installations of the integration solution, for environment
addresses, see Section 9.2.1

## Page 380


380
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

5. INPUT AND OUTPUT DATA
5.1.
Nature, Arrangement and Initial Preparation of Input and Output
Data
5.1.1. Information Sources
The main information sources for the system are:
1) Related information systems involved in both infocommunication and functional
exchange.
2) Data entered by system users.
Operations on:
1) analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog signal conversion;
2) optical character recognition;
3) other actions to bring information to a form suitable for computer processing
are not provided for as part of the OMS-Cloud 4.0 AS functions.
5.1.2. Methods of Data Collection, Transmission, Control and Correction Arrangement
The information arrays are collected during the system operation through:
1) Receiving of structured XML-documents generated on the basis of specified XSD-
schemes via collaborative services.
2) User input of information in screen forms and its subsequent storage in the database.
Data integrity control is implemented by the system application software and tools built in
the used DBMS (restrictions, indices, primary and secondary keys). Data entry and correction
shall be carried out only through the system software components. Direct user access to the
database is not expected.
The main requirements to the processes of data collection, transmission, control and
correction arrangement are to ensure the reliability, verifiability, confidentiality, accessibility,
efficiency of the data collected and transmitted.
The reliability requirement implies arrangement of the information collection and
transmission process, within the scope of which the transmitted and collected information will not
be subject to misrepresentation.
The verifiability requirement implies arrangement of the information collection and
transmission process which allows control over the reliability of the transmitted information.
The confidentiality requirement means provision of access to information in strict
adherence to the established access isolation priorities and rules.


---

## Page 381


381
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

The accessibility requirement means the fundamental possibility of obtaining the collected
information and its transfer.
The efficiency requirement implies such arrangement of the information collection and
transmission process, during which the available information will be transmitted in a time frame
acceptable for its analysis.


---

## Page 382


382
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

5.2.
Format, Description and Encoding Method of the Input and
Output Data when Using API
Format, description and encoding method of the input and output data when using API
are provided in description of the relevant methods.

biologically active food additives” goods group is available for the third wave of payment
(paymentGroup=3 in a goods card) from October 1, 2024 to August 31, 2026.

available for the tenth payment wave (paymentGroup=10 in a goods card) from March
31, 2026 to February 28, 2027.

Notes:
1. Pack-template lacks AI in template and MC.
2. templateId=2 is removed for “Medicines for human use” goods group, this template is
no longer applicable for marking codes emission, as well as the MCs emitted by
templateId=2 cannot be specified in the reports on utilization (application). The template
with code “5” shall be used instead.
3. templateId=6 is removed for goods group “Dairy products”, this template is not used
anymore for marking codes emission, but marking codes, which have been emitted by
this template, are supported in the reports.
4.  templateId=18 with 13-symbol serial number is no longer applicable for emission of the
marking codes for the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages” goods group.
Emission is only available for templateId=18 with 7-symbol serial number.  MCs, which
have been emitted in line with this template with 13-symbol serial number, are not
supported in the reports.
5. templateId=34 with 6-symbol serial number is no longer applicable for emission of the
marking codes for the “Games and toys for children” goods group. Emission is only
available for templateId=34 with 13-symbol serial number. MCs, which have been
emitted in line with this template with 6-symbol serial number, are not supported in the
reports.
6. templateId=56 with 6-symbol serial number is no longer applicable for emission of the
marking codes for the “Pyrotechnic articles and fire safety and firefighting equipment”
goods group. Emission is only available for templateId=56 with 13-symbol serial
number. MCs, which have been emitted in line with this template with 6-symbol serial


---

## Page 391


391
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

number, are not supported in the reports.
7. From October 1, 2025, templateId=70 with 6-character serial number will no longer be
valid for issuance of marking codes for the “Radio electronic products. Electronic
nicotine delivery systems” goods group. From October 1, 2025, issuance will be
available only for templateId=70 with 13-character serial number, issuance with the use
of templateId=71 will remain unchanged. From October 1, 2025, MCs issued using
templateId=70 with 6-character serial number, will no longer be supported in the
reports.
8. For the “Radio-electronic products” goods group, templateId=36 and templateId=37
with a 13-character serial number will not be applicable for marking code issue as of
January 1, 2026. Starting on this date, issue for templateId=36 and templateId=37 will
be available only with a 20-character serial number. MCs issued under templates of
templateId=36 and templateId=37 with a 13-character serial number, will no longer be
supported in reports as of January 1, 2026.

Notes:
1. The cancellation takes place the day after the expiration date at 00:00h (in case of the
“Footwear” goods group, the cancellation will be at 00:00h on the 61st business day).
2. When payment type is “Payment upon emission” for the "Antiseptic/antibacterial skin
cleansers and hand sanitizers", "Specialized food products and biologically active food
additives", “Medical products”, “Dairy products", "Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol
beverages", "Packaged water", “Pet foods”, “Canned foods”, “Vegetable oils”, “Veterinary
medicines”, “Non-alcoholic beer”, “Seafood” and “Juice products and non-alcoholic
beverages” goods groups, MC lifetime is not calculated, the codes have no expiration date.
3. For the "Antiseptic/antibacterial skin cleansers and hand sanitizers", "Specialized food
products and biologically active food additives”, "Packaged water", “Pet foods”, “Canned
foods”, “Vegetable oils”, “Veterinary medicines”, “Non-alcoholic beer”, “Seafood” and
“Juice products and non-alcoholic beverages” goods groups, MC expiration date is
calculated for all roles of the service provider which are available for the goods group.
4. For “Footwear”, “Light industry”, “Perfume and eau de toilette”, “New pneumatic rubber
tires and tire casings”, “Photo cameras (except cine cameras), flash lights and flash bulbs”
goods groups, MC lifetime is 90 days both in case of MC application w/o assistance and
in case of application of the MCs by a service provider.
5. MC cancellation will be launched for the “Radio-electronic products” goods group from May
1, 2026.
6. MC cancellation will be launched for the “Real fur” goods group from March 1, 2026.
7. MC cancellation will be launched for the “Meat products” goods group from August 1, 2026.
8. MC cancellation will be launched for the “Confectionery products” goods group from March
1, 2026.


---

## Page 404


404
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

9. MC cancellation will be launched for the “Radio-electronic products. Laptops and
smartphones” goods group from May 1, 2026.

Table 278 – Codes of the result of processing of the marking codes on the
application report and rejection report (for all goods groups, except "Alternative tobacco
products", "Nicotine products" and "Tobacco products”)
Value
Descirption
Explanation
0
Ok
Code processing has been successfully
completed
1
Duplicate
The code specified is a duplicate.
Possible situations:
1. Emission of the same code has been
requested.
2. One code has been specified several
times in the application report.
3. Several identical reports have been
received.
Code has already been utilised
Code has already been applied.
CIS
has
already
been
dropped_out
Code has already been written off.
2
Not exists KM
The code specified is missing in the system.
Possible situations:
1. GG, which does not correspond to the
goods group of the marking code is
specified
2. Emission for the code is missing
3. Incorrect code format is specified
There is no emission for code
Code emission is missing (only for the report
on withdrawal of the MC without application
of the MC if report processing failed).
3
Report not valid tags
Incorrect (invalid) data were specified for the
report
4
Written off KM
Incorrect code status (code has already
been written off - it is used in case of
application report)
6
Insufficient funds on account
Insufficient funds
7
Billing contract not found
Personal account was not found
8
Payment type can't be resolved
Incorrect tariff type was specified
9
Owner is not correct
GCP is not the MC owner

7. OMS API 3.0 USE EXAMPLES
This section contains OMS API 3.0 use examples.
7.1.
Signing Requests with the Electronic Digital Signature
This subsection describes the method of signing an HTTP request using the example of
CryptoPro JCP library from CRYPTO-PRO Limited Liability Company. When using a library of
other crypto provider, follow the guidelines for the selected library.
Before using other libraries, make sure that the library supports the required standards
which can be found in subsection 2.3.
7.1.1. Library “CryptoPro JCP”
To get the CryptoPro JCP library, go through the registration procedure on the official
website of CRYPTO-PRO Limited Liability Company (https://www.cryptopro.ru/). After
registration, go to the "Download Center" section and select the required library from the list, in
our case it is "CryptoPro JCP" (Figure 232).


---

## Page 416


416
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

List of CryptoPro Software Products
Figure 232
After clicking on the "CryptoPro JCP" link, the user gets the list of library versions available
for downloading (Figure 233).


---

## Page 417


417
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

List of “CryptoPro JCP” Versions
Figure 233
After downloading the selected version, get access on your computer to archive "jcp-
2.0.40035.zip" with the library and examples of use (see Figure 234).


---

## Page 418


418
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Content of “CryptoPro JCP” Library
Figure 234
The “sample-sources” directory contains a subdirectory “CMS_samples”, which provides
an example of classes and functions to generate a detached signature of CMS format [IETF RFC
5652] (Figure 235).


---

## Page 419


419
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Examples of “CryptoPro JCP” Use
Figure 235
The next section provides an example of calling a function to sign a request using
detached signature in CMS format [IETF RFC 5652]; the requirements for request signing are
given in section 2.3.1.
7.1.2. Example with the Use of Library to Sign the Request
Below is an example of using “CryptoPro JCP” library using classes from the examples
distributed with the library.
Note: The program code in the examples distributed with the library is intended for training
purposes. This code cannot be directly used to protect information. Crypto-Pro company does not
bear any responsibility for the operation of this code. (C) Crypto-Pro, LLC 2004-2007.


---

## Page 420


420
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

To sign the request, the data must be converted into a byte array and sent to
CMS.CMSSignEx signature method of the library, see the method parameters below (Table 284).
To generate a detached signature, parameter “detached” shall contain value “true”.
Table 284 - Parameters of CMS Format Signature Generation Function
Description
/**
 * sign CMS
 *
 * @param data - Signed data array
 * @param key - Private key
 * @param cert cert - Certificate
 * @param detached - Detached signature use attribute
 * @param digestOid - OID of hashing algorithm
 * @param signOid - OID of signature algorithm
 * @param signAlg – Signature algorithm name
 * @param providerName – Provider name;
 * @throws Exception e
 */
public static byte[] CMSSignEx(byte[] data, PrivateKey key,
 Certificate cert, boolean detached, String digestOid,
 String signOid, String signAlg, String providerName)
 throws Exception {

 // sign
 final Signature signature = Signature.getInstance(signAlg, providerName);
 signature.initSign(key);
 signature.update(data);

 final byte[] sign = signature.sign();

 // create cms format
 return createCMSEx(data, sign, cert, detached, digestOid, signOid);
}
The function execution result is the signature of CMS format [IETF RFC 5652], the
generated signature of the message request in CMS format fit in the HTTP header in “X-
Signature” parameter in Base64 encoding.
Verification of the generated request signature fit in HTTP in “X-Signature” parameter can
be performed with the support program use of cURL command-line designed to transfer data
using cURL syntax.
An example of command used to verify the request signature is shown below (Table 185).
Example of Command to Verify the Request Signature:
curl -X POST "https://intuot.crpt.ru:12011/api/v3/orders?omsId=" -H
"@headers.txt" -H "@XSignature.txt" --data "@order.json"
PAUSE >nul


---

## Page 421


421
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Where:
1) URL: address of the resource where the request is sent;
2) "@headers.txt" – file containing the HTTP header of the request (excluding “X-
Signature” parameter);
3) "@XSignature.txt" – file containing parameter “X-Signature” from the request HTTP
header;
4) "@order.json" – file containing the request data.

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

8. STRUCTURES AND FORMATS OF MARKING CODES,
IDENTIFICATION CODES, AND VERIFICATION CODES
8.1.
“Tobacco products”
8.1.1. Consumer package (non GS1)
The identification means for consumer package of tobacco products contains a marking
code that includes 4 groups of data, where the first 3 groups form an identification code for
consumer package of tobacco products, the fourth group comprises a verification code, while:
the first group consists of 14 digits and contains the goods code;
the second group consists of 7 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters
of the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and contains the identification code for the
package of tobacco products;
the third group consists of 4 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters of
the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and contains maximum retail price of the
consumer package (in recoded format) with a precision of one kopeck;
the fourth group consists of 4 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters of
the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and contains the verification code.
Table 285 – Marking Code Structure
Code
Length
Format
Type

14
Figures
Goods code according to the corresponding
goods nomenclature (GTIN)

7

Tobacco products package identification code
3
4

Maximum retail price of consumer package
with an accuracy of one kopeck in recoded
form
4
4
Symbol string
Verification code

8.1.2. Group package (GS1)

The identification means for the group packing of tobacco products contains a marking
code that includes 4 mandatory data groups, where the first 3 groups form the group packing
identification code, the fourth group comprises a verification code, while:
1. the first group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '01' and
contains the product code (14 digits) of the group packing;


---

## Page 423


423
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

2. the second group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '21'
and contains the individual serial number (7 characters) of the group package. The
end symbol for this data group is a special divider with code 29 in the table of ASCII
symbols.
3. the third group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '8005'
and contains the sum of the maximum retail prices of the enclosed consumer
packages, expressed in kopecks, with an accuracy of one kopeck (6 characters).
The end symbol for this data group is a special divider with code 29 in the table of
ASCII symbols.
4. the fourth group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '93'
and contains a verification code (4 characters).

8.2.
“Light industry”
The identification means for consumer goods (consumer package, consumer goods
packages and sets) contains a marking code that includes 4 groups of data, where the first and
second groups form an identification code of the consumer package or a package identification
code, or a set identification code, the third and fourth groups form a verification code, while:
the first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an application
identifier (01), generated by the operator based on the information submitted by a consumer goods
circulation participant;
the second group consists of 13 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters
of the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and contains an individual serial number of a
goods unit preceded by an application identifier (21) and which is generated by an operator or a
consumer goods circulation participant. The terminating character for this data group must be a
separator that has code 29 in the ASCII character table. When generating serial numbers
independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 12 characters. The country
code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the received serial


---

## Page 424


424
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

number.the third group is 4 characters long and contains a verification key, preceded by an
application identifier (91) and generated by the operator. The terminating character for this data
group must be a special delimiter character with code 29 in the ASCII character table;
the fourth group consists of 44 characters (numbers, uppercase and lowercase letters
of the Latin alphabet and special characters) and contains a verification code, preceded by an
application identifier (92) and generated by the operator.

8.3.
“Footwear”
The identification means for footwear contains a marking code that includes 4 data
groups, where the first and second groups form an identification code, the third and fourth groups
form a verification code, while:
the first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an application
identifier (01), generated by the operator based on the information submitted by a footwear goods
circulation participant;
the second group consists of 13 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters
of the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and contains an individual serial number of a
goods unit, which is preceded by an application identifier (21) and which is generated by an
operator or footwear goods circulation participant. The terminating character for this data group
is the delimiter character code 29 in the ASCII character table. When generating serial numbers
independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 12 characters. The country
code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the received serial number.
the third group consists of 4 characters and contains an identifier (individual sequence
number) of the verification key, preceded by an application identifier (91) and generated by the
operator. The terminating character for this data group is a special delimiter character code 29 in
the ASCII character table;
the fourth group consists of 88 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters
of the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and contains the value of the verification
code, preceded by an application identifier (92) and generated by the operator.

The “Tires” goods identification means contains a marking code that includes 4 data

the first group consists of 14 digits and contains a product code, preceded by an
application identifier (01), and which is generated by the operator or a “Tires” goods circulation
participant based on the information submitted by the a “Tires” goods circulation participant;
the second group consists of 13 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters

“Tires” goods circulation participant. The terminating character for this data group must be a
separator that has code 29 in the ASCII character table. When generating serial numbers
independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 12 characters. The country
code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the received serial number.
the third group is 4 characters long and contains a verification key, preceded by an
application identifier (91) and generated by the operator. The terminating character for this data
group must be a special delimiter character with code 29 in the ASCII character table;
the fourth group consists of 44 characters (numbers, uppercase and lowercase letters
of the Latin alphabet and special characters) and contains a verification code, preceded by an
application identifier (92) and generated by the operator.

8.5.
“Perfume and Eau de Toilette”
Identification means of the perfumery consumer package and perfumery set contain a
marking code, which includes 4 data groups of which the first two groups comprise an
identification code of the consumer goods or identification code of the goods set, and the third
and the fourth groups comprise a verification code, while:
1) The first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an
application identifier (01), is generated by the operator based on information submitted
by a perfumery products circulation participant.
2) The second group consists of 13 symbols (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains individual serial number of the goods item
preceded by the application identifier (21) and that is generated by the operator or the
perfumery products circulation participant. The end symbol for this data group should be
a divider that has a code 29 in ASCII symbol table.
When generating serial numbers independently, the Participant shall add a serial number
consisting of 19 characters. The country code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be
entered before the received serial number.
3) The third group is 4 characters long and contains a verification key, preceded by an
application identifier (91) and generated by the operator. The terminating character for
this data group must be a special delimiter character with code 29 in the ASCII character
table;
4) The fourth group consists of 44 characters (numbers, uppercase and lowercase letters
of the Latin alphabet and special characters) and contains a verification code, preceded
by an application identifier (92) and generated by the operator.

“Photo cameras (except cine cameras), flash lights and flash

Identification means of photo products (of consumer package and sets) contain a marking
code, which includes 4 data groups of which the first and the second groups comprise an
identification code of the consumer package or identification code of the set, the third and the
fourth groups comprise a verification code, while:
1) The first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an
application identifier (01), is generated by the operator based on information submitted
by a photo products circulation participant.
2) The second group consists of 20 symbols (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains individual serial number of the goods item
preceded by the application identifier (21) and that is generated by the operator or a photo
products circulation participant. The end symbol for this data group should be a divider
that has a code 29 in ASCII symbol table.
When generating serial numbers independently, the Participant shall add a serial
number consisting of 19 characters. The country code is indicated by the Emission Server
and shall be indicated before the received serial number.
3) The third group consists of 4 symbols and contains a verification key preceded by the
application identifier (91) and that is generated by the operator. The end symbol for this
data group should be a special divider that has a code 29 in ASCII symbol table.
4) The fourth group consists of 44 symbols (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains a verification code preceded by the
application identifier (92) and that is generated by the operator.
