8.7.
“Medicines for human use”
The identification means for medicines contains a marking code that includes 4 groups of
data, where the first and second groups form an identification code, the third and fourth groups
form a verification code, while:
the first data group is the global trade unit number, consisting of 14 numeric characters,
preceded by an application identifier (01);
the second group of data is an individual serial number of a trade unit, consisting of 13
characters of a numeric or alphanumeric sequence (Latin alphabet), preceded by an application
identifier (21). The terminating character for this data group is a special delimiter character with
code 29 in the ASCII character table or FUNCTION 1 (FNC1);
the third group of data is the identifier (individual serial number) of the verification key
provided to the issuers of identification means by the operator of the monitoring system as part
of the verification code, consisting of 4 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters of
the Latin alphabet), preceded by the application identifier (91). The terminating character for this
data group is a special delimiter character with code 29 in the ASCII character table or FUNCTION
1 (FNC1);
the fourth data group — the value of the verification code provided to the issuers of
identification means by the operator of the monitoring system as part of the verification code,
which is preceded by the application identifier (92), and consisting of 44 characters (numbers,
lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters).

8.8.
“Dairy products”
Identification means of the dairy products (of consumer package, group package and sets)
contain a marking code, which includes 4 data groups of which the first two groups comprise an
identification code of the consumer package or identification code of the group package, or
identification code of the sets, the third group comprises a verification code, the fourth group
comprises an actual weight of the product, while:


---

## Page 429


429
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

the first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code;
the second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 6
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters) and contains the individual serial number of dairy products packing and ends with
the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29);
When generating serial numbers independently, the Participant shall add a serial
number consisting of 5 characters. The country code is indicated by the Emission Server and
shall be entered before the received serial number.
the third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters) and contains the verification code.
the fourth group is identified by the application identifier AI=’3103’, it consists of 6
digits and contains an actual weight of products in kilograms. This group is optional and can
be used only if reports on MC application are sent.

the first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an application
identifier (01), generated by the operator of the information system based on the information
submitted by the goods circulation participant;
the second group consists of 13 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters
of the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and contains an individual serial number of a

unit of goods, which is preceded by an application identifier (21) and which is generated by an

consisting of 12 characters. A country code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be
entered before the received serial number.

8.10. "Medical products"
Identification means of medical products contain a marking code (of the consumer
package, group package or sets) including 4 data groups, the first and the second groups
comprise an identification code of the consumer package, group package or sets, the third and
the fourth groups comprise a verification code, while:
The first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an
application identifier (01), is generated by the operator of the information system based on
information submitted by a goods circulation participant.
The second group consists of 13 symbols (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin letters,
and special characters) and contains individual serial number of the goods item preceded by the
application identifier (21) and that is generated by the operator or a goods circulation participant.
The end symbol for this data group is a special divider with code 29 in the table of ASCII symbols.


---

431
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

the third group is 4 characters long and contains a verification key, preceded by an application
identifier (91) and generated by the operator. The terminating character for this data group is a
special delimiter character code 29 in the ASCI character table. When generating serial numbers
independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 12 characters. The country
code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the received serial number.
When generating serial numbers independently, the Participant shall add a serial number
consisting of 12 characters. The country code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be
entered before the received serial number.
the fourth group, the fourth group consists of 44 characters (numbers, lowercase and

code preceded by an application identifier (92) and generated by the operator of the information
system.

8.11. “Alternative tobacco products”
8.11.1. Consumer package (non GS1)
The consumer package identification means contains a marking code that includes 4 data
groups, where the first 3 groups form the consumer package identification code, the fourth group
comprises a verification code, while:
the first group consists of 14 digits and contains the goods code;
the second group consists of 7 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters
of the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and contains an individual serial number of a
package of tobacco products;
the third group consists of 4 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters of
the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and contains, in recoded form, the maximum
retail price of consumer package with an accuracy of one kopeck, while the price is indicated
equal to zero rubles and zero kopecks;


---

## Page 432


432
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

the fourth group consists of 4 characters (numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters of
the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and contains the verification code.

8.11.2. Consumer package (GS1)
The consumer package identification means contains a marking code that includes 3
mandatory data groups, where the first 2 groups form the consumer package identification code,
the third group comprises a verification code, while:
the first group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '01' and
contains the product code (14 digits) of the consumer packing;
the second group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '21' and
contains an individual serial number (7 characters) of the consumer packaging;
the third group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '8005' and
contains maximum retail price of the consumer package with a precision of one kopeck. At the
same time the maximum retail price equal to 000000 (six zeros) is specified in the third group of
data;
the fourth group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '93' and
contains a verification code (4 characters).

8.11.3. Group package (GS1)
The group packing identification means contains a marking code that includes 3
mandatory data groups, where the first 2 groups form the group packing identification code, the

the first group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '01' and

retail prices of consumer packages equal to 000000 (six zeros) is specified in the third group of
data;
the fourth group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '93' and
contains a verification code (4 characters).

8.12. “Packed water”
The identification means for packed water, including natural mineral water, contains a
marking code (of consumer package and group package) that includes 3 data groups, where the
first 2 groups form consumer and group package identification code while:
the first group is identified by the application identifier AI = '01', consists of 14 digits and
contains the goods code;
the second group is identified by the application identifier AI = '21', consists of 13
characters (numbers, uppercase and lowercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters) and contains the individual serial number of the drinking water package, including
natural mineral water and ends with the separator character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating
serial numbers independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 12


---

434
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

characters. The country code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the
received serial number;
the third group is identified by the application identifier AI = '93', consists of 4 characters
(numbers, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters)
and contains a verification code.

8.13. “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages”
The identification means of beer-based beverages contain a marking code (of consumer
package and group package) including four data groups, the first two of which generate the
identification code of consumer and group package, the third group comprises a verification code,
the fourth group comprises a volume of the products, while:
the first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits and
contains a goods code;
the second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 7
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters) and contains the individual serial number of beer-based beverages packing and ends
with the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29).
When generating serial numbers independently, the Participant shall add a serial number
consisting of 6 characters. A country code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be
entered before the received serial number.
The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4 characters
(digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special characters) and
contains a verification code.
The fourth group is identified by the application identifier AI=’335Y’ (allowed values are
AI='3350', '3351', '3352', '3353', '3354', '3355'), it consists of 6 digits and contains a volume of the
products in liters. This group is optional and can be used only if reports on MC application are
sent.

8.14. “Nicotine products”
8.14.1. Group package (GS1)
Identification means of nicotine products group package contain a marking code including
four mandatory data groups, the first three of which comprise a group package identification code,
the fourth group comprises a verification code, while:
1) The first group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = ’01’ and
contains a goods code (14 digits) of the group packing.
2) The second group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = ’21’, it
contains an individual serial number (7 characters) of the group packing.
3) The third group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = '8005' and
contains the sum of the maximum retail prices of the nested consumer packages
expressed in kopecks, with a precision of one kopeck (6 digits). At the same time the sum
of the maximum retail prices of the consumer packages equals to 000000 (six zeros) is
specified in the third group of data.
4) The fourth group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = ’93’ and
contains a verification code (4 characters).

8.15. “Specialized food products and biologically active food
additives”
Identification means of the specialized food products and biologically active food additives
contain a marking code (of consumer and group packages, and sets), which includes 3 data
groups of which the first two groups comprise an identification code of the consumer package or
identification code of the group package, or identification code of the sets, the third group
comprises a verification code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code;
2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 13
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the individual serial number of specialized food products
and biologically active food additives packing and ends with the delimiter character FNC1
(ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers independently, the Participant shall add a
serial number consisting of 12 characters. The country code is indicated by the Emission
Server and shall be entered before the received serial number;
3) The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the verification code.

8.17. “Non-alcoholic beer”
Identification means of non-alcoholic beer products contain a marking code (of the
consumer or group package or sets) including 3 mandatory data groups, the first 2 of which
generate an identification code of the consumer or group package or sets, the third group
comprises a verification code, the fourth group comprises a volume of the products while:
1) The first group of mandatory data is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it
consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code;
2) The second group of mandatory data is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’,
it consists of 7 characters and contains an individual serial number of beer beverages
packing. The end symbol for this data group is a special divider with code 29 in the table
of ASCII symbols. When generating serial numbers independently, the Participant shall
add a serial number consisting of 6 characters. The country code is indicated by the
Emission Server and shall be entered before the received serial number.
3) The third group of mandatory data is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it
consists of 4 characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as
well as special characters) and contains a verification code.
4) The fourth group is identified by the application identifier AI=’335Y’ (allowed values
are AI='3350', '3351', '3352', '3353', '3354', '3355'), it consists of 6 digits and contains a
volume of the products in liters. This group is optional and can be used only if reports on
MC application are sent.

8.18. “Juice products and non-alcoholic beverages”
Identification means of juice products and non-alcoholic beverages contain a consumer
package marking code including 3 mandatory data groups, the first 2 of which generate an
identification code of the consumer package, the third group comprises a verification code, while:
1) the first group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = ’01’ and
contains a goods code (14 digits) of the consumer and group package;
2) the second group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = ’21’ and
contains an individual serial number (13 characters) of the consumer package. The end
symbol for this data group is a special divider with code 29 in the table of ASCII symbols.
When generating serial numbers independently, the Participant shall add a serial number
consisting of 12 characters. The country code is indicated by the Emission Server and
shall be entered before the received serial number;
3) the third group of mandatory data is identified by the application code AI = ’93’ and
contains a verification code (4 characters).

8.19. “Seafood”
Identification means of seafood contain a marking code (of consumer, group packages and
sets), which includes 3 data groups of which the first two groups comprise an identification code
of the consumer package, identification code of the group package or a set, the third group
comprises a verification code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code.
2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 6
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the individual serial number of the seafood package and
ends with the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers
independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 5 characters. A
country code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the received
serial number.
3) The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the verification code.
Note: actual weight of the unit of products can be indicated in the marking code structure
after the third data group (after a verification code with indication of symbol ASCII 29) by using
application identifier AI = '3103'. Indication of the actual weight after the third data group is only
available to transfer weight to the system as part of information of the application report for the


---

## Page 443


443
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

goods that have an attribute of variable weight. However, adding weight information into a
structure of the MC for its further conversion into identification means is not allowed

Identification means of pet food products contain a marking code (of the consumer, group
packages and sets) including 4 data groups, the first and the second groups generate an
identification code of the consumer, group packages and sets, the third and the fourth groups

1) The first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an

3) The third group consists of 4 symbols and contains a verification key that is preceded

Identification means of pet food products contain a marking code (of the consumer, group
packages and sets) including 3 data groups, the first two groups generate an identification code
of the consumer, group packages and sets, the third group comprises a verification code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code.


---

## Page 446


446
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 6
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the individual serial number of the package and ends
with the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers
independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 5 characters. The
country code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the received
serial number.
3) The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the verification code.

Note: the actual weight of the unit of products can be indicated in the marking code
structure after the third data group (after a verification code with indication of symbol ASCII 29)
by using application identifier AI = '3103'. Indication of the actual weight after the third data group
is applicable only to transfer weight to the system as part of information of the application report
for the goods that have an attribute of variable weight. However, adding weight information into a
structure of the MC for its further conversion into identification means is not allowed.


---

8.21. “Games and toys for children”
Identification means of games and toys for children contain a marking code (of the
consumer or group package or a set) including 4 data groups, the first and the second groups
generate an identification code of the consumer or group package or a set, the third and the fourth
groups comprise a verification code, while:
1) The first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an
application identifier (01), is generated by the Operator of the information system based
on information submitted by a circulation participant.
2) The second group consists of 13 symbols (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains individual serial number of the goods item
that is preceded by the application identifier (21) and that is generated by the operator or
a circulation participant. The end symbol for this data group is a special divider with code
29 in the table of ASCII symbols.
When generating serial numbers independently, the Participant shall add a serial
number consisting of 12 symbols. The country code is indicated by the Emission Server
and shall be entered before the received serial number.
3) The third group consists of 4 symbols and contains a verification key that is preceded
by the application identifier (91) and that is generated by the operator. The end symbol
for this data group is a special divider with code 29 in the table of ASCII symbols.
4) The fourth group consists of 44 symbols (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains a verification code that is preceded by the
application identifier (92) and that is generated by the operator of the information system.


---

8.22. “Titanium metal products”
The identification means of titanium metal products contain a marking code (of the
consumer package) including 3 data groups, the first 2 of which generate an identification code
of the consumer package, the third group comprises a verification code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code.
2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 13
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the individual serial number of the package and ends
with the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers
independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 12 characters. The
country code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the received
serial number.
3) The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as

8.23. “Radio-electronic products”
Identification means of radio-electronic products contain a marking code (of the consumer
package, group package or set) including 3 data groups, the first 2 of which generate an
identification code of the consumer, group package or set, the third group comprises a verification
code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code.
2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 20
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the individual serial number of the package and ends
with the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers
independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 19 characters. The
country code is indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the received
serial number.
3) The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the verification code.
4) The fourth group is identified by one of following application identifiers:
− AI=’30’ (it consists of 1 to 8 digits);
− AI=’310Y’ (allowed values are AI='3100', '3101', '3102', '3103', '3104', '3105', it consists
of 6 digits);
− AI=’311Y’ (allowed values are AI='3110', '3111', '3112', '3113', '3114', '3115', it consists
of 6 digits);
− AI=’335Y’ (allowed values are AI='3350', '3351', '3352', '3353', '3354', '3355', it consists
of 6 digits);
This group is optional and can be used only when reports on MC application are sent.
Only one variable characteristic can be specified for one marking code.
However, adding weight information to the structure of the MC for its subsequent
conversion into identification means is not allowed.


---

93 Verification code

Identification means of building materials contains a marking code (of consumer or group
package), which includes 4 data groups of which the first and the second groups generate an
identification code of the consumer or group package, the third and the fourth groups generate a
verification code, while:
1) The first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an
application identifier (01), is generated by the Operator of the information system based
on information submitted by a circulation participant.
2) The second group consists of 13 symbols (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains an individual serial number of the goods item
preceded by the application identifier (21) and that is generated by the operator or a
circulation participant. The end symbol for this data group is a special divider with code
29 in the table of ASCII symbols. When generating serial numbers independently, a
Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 12 characters. A country code is
indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the received serial number.
3) The third group consists of 4 symbols and contains a verification key preceded by the
application identifier (91) and that is generated by the operator. The end symbol for this
data group is a special divider with code 29 in the table of ASCI symbols.
4) The fourth group consists of 44 symbols (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains a verification code preceded by the
application identifier (92) and that is generated by the operator of the information system.


---

8.30. “Pyrotechnic articles and fire safety and firefighting equipment”
Identification means contains a marking code (of consumer package, group package or
set) which includes 3 data groups of which the first two groups generate an identification code of
the consumer package, group package or set, the third group generates a verification code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code.
2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 6
symbols (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters) and contains the individual serial number of the package and ends with the
delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers independently, a
Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 5 characters. A country code is
indicated by the Emission Server and shall be entered before the received serial number.
3) The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4 symbols
(digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as special
characters) and contains a verification code.

91
Verification key
4
44
Symbol string
92
Verification code

8.39. “Radio-electronic
products.
Electronic
nicotine
delivery
systems”
Identification means contain a marking code (of a consumer package, group package or a
set) that includes 3 data groups of which the first two groups generate an identification code of
the consumer or group package, the third group generates a verification code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code.
2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 13
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains an individual serial number of the package and ends
with the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers
independently, a Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 12 characters. A
country code is indicated by the Emission Server and is placed before the received serial
number.
3) The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the verification code.


---

including 4 data groups, the first and the second groups generate an identification code of the
consumer or group package, the third and the fourth groups generate a verification code, while:
1) The first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an
application identifier (01), is generated by the Operator of the information system based
on the information submitted by a circulation participant.
2) The second group consists of 13 characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains an individual serial number of the goods item
preceded by the application identifier (21) and that is generated by the operator or a
circulation participant. The end symbol for this data group is a special divider with code
29 in the table of ASCII characters. When generating serial numbers independently, a
Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 12 characters. A country code is
indicated by the Emission Server and is placed before the received serial number.
3) The third group consists of 4 characters and contains a verification key that is
preceded by the application identifier (91) and is generated by the operator. The end
character for this data group is a special divider with code 29 in the table of ASCI symbols.
4) The fourth group consists of 44 characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains a verification code that is preceded by the
application identifier (92) and is generated by the operator of the information system.

8.40. “Radio-electronic products. Laptops and smartphones”
Identification means contain a marking code (of the consumer package, group package or
set), which includes 3 data groups of which the first two groups generate an identification code of
the consumer or group package, the third group generates a verification code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code.
2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 20
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains an individual serial number of the package and ends
with the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers
independently, a Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 19 characters. A
country code is indicated by the Emission Server and is indicated before the received
serial number.
3) The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains a verification code.

preceded by the application identifier (21) and that is generated by the operator or a

8.41. “Meat products”
Identification means contain a marking code (of the consumer, group package, or set),
which includes 3 data groups of which the first two groups constitute an identification code of the
consumer or group package, the third group constitutes a verification code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code.
2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 6
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the individual serial number of the package and ends
with the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers
independently, a Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 5 characters. A
country code is indicated by the Emission Server and is indicated before the received
serial number.
3) The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains the verification code.

Note: the actual weight of the product unit may be indicated in the marking code structure
after the third data group (after the verification code with indication of symbol ASCII 29) by using
application identifier AI = '3103'. Indication of the actual weight after the third data group is
permitted only for transmitting weight information to the system as part of the application report
for the goods that have an attribute of variable weight. However, adding weight data into the MC
structure for its subsequent conversion into identification means is not permitted.


---

8.46. “Semi-finished and frozen food products”
Identification means contain a marking code (of the consumer package, group package
and set) which includes 3 data groups of which the first two groups comprise an identification
code, the third group comprise a verification code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code.
2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 6
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains an individual serial number of the package and ends
with the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers
independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 5 characters. A

serial number.

characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains a verification code.

Note: the actual weight of a product unit may be indicated in the marking code structure
after the third data group (after the verification code with indication of symbol ASCII 29) using
application identifier AI = '3103'. Indication of the actual weight after the third data group is
permitted solely to transfer weight data to the system within the application report for the goods
that have an attribute of variable weight. Adding weight information to the structure of the MC for
its subsequent conversion into identification means is not allowed.

8.48. “Pyrotechnic articles”
Identification means contain a marking code (of the consumer package, group package,
and set) which includes 3 data groups of which the first two groups comprise an identification
code, the third group comprises a verification code, while:
1) The first group is identified by the application identifier AI=’01’, it consists of 14 digits
and contains a goods code.
2) The second group is identified by the application identifier AI=’21’, it consists of 6
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains an individual serial number of the package and ends
with the delimiter character FNC1 (ASCII 29). When generating serial numbers
independently, the Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 5 characters. A
country code is indicated by the Emission Server and is indicated before the received
serial number.
3) The third group is identified by the application identifier AI=’93’, it consists of 4
characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase letters of the Latin alphabet, as well as
special characters) and contains a verification code.


---

499
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Format

1

Figures

string

3

string
93 Verification code

Identification means contain a marking code (of the consumer, group package, and set)
including 4 data groups, the first and the second groups comprise an identification code, the third
and the forth groups comprise a verification code, while:
1) The first group consists of 14 digits and contains a goods code preceded by an
application identifier (01), is generated by the Operator of the information system based
on the information submitted by a circulation participant.
2) The second group consists of 13 characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains an individual serial number of the goods item
that is preceded by the application identifier (21) and is generated by the operator or a
circulation participant. The end symbol for this data group is a special divider with code
29 in the table of ASCII symbols. When generating serial numbers independently, the
Participant shall add a serial number consisting of 12 characters. A country code is
indicated by the Emission Server and is indicated before the received serial number.
3) The third group consists of 4 characters and contains a verification key that is
preceded by the application identifier (91) and is generated by the operator. The end
symbol for this data group is a special divider with code 29 in the table of ASCI symbols.
4) The fourth group consists of 44 characters (digits, lowercase and uppercase Latin
letters, and special characters) and contains a verification code that is preceded by the
application identifier (92) and is generated by the operator of the information system.

## Page 501


501
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

9. AUTHORIZATION AND AUTHENTICATION

9.1.
Getting a client token
To increase the security of information interaction, a mechanism for getting a client token
by applying to single authentication methods has been implemented:
1)   Obtaining a unique connection identifier (registration of external connection –
omsConnection).
1. A connection identifier is required to install integration solution. A process of
integration solution installation registration is described in section 9.1.1.
2. In the transitional period, a connection identifier can be obtained in two ways:
(1) By registering the installation of the integration solution via API (see subsection
9.2).
(2) Via the OMS user interface.
2) Getting a client token.
1. For each installation of the integration solution, only one client token is available
(getting a client token by applying to single authentication methods of the TT GIS (TT
GIS API / True API) is described in section 9.1.2 and getting a client token of the
MDLP IS (for a GCP with the “Medicines for medical use” goods group) is described
in section 9.1.3)
2. If a client token is requested repeatedly for the same installation, the previous token
terminates and a new token is generated.
3. Operation time of the client token:
(1) 10 hours for tokens received via TT GIS API / True API.
(2) is specified in a response when the token is provided (for tokens received via
API of the MDLP IS).
3) Request of data in the OMS API using a client token.
1. It is necessary to use a received client token to make requests to the OMS API.
2. After the expiration of the client token, it is repeatedly obtained according to the
procedure described in item 2.


---

## Page 502


502
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

9.1.1. Process “01.00.00.00 Registration of installation of integration decision in the
OMS”
Diagram of the registration process for installation of the integration decision in the OMS by
means
of
OMS
API
can
be
found
in
Figure
236.

Process “Registration of installation of integration decision in the OMS”
Figure 236
Description:
1) APCS forms a request to register the installation of integration decision and sends it
to the OMS.
2) The OMS verifies the request.
− If request contains errors, the OMS will register the error in the log;


---

## Page 503


503
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

− The OMS generates an error message and sends it to APCS;
− Then you will be taken to step 5) of the main scenario.
3) If there are no errors, the OMS will register the installation of the integration decision
and will assign to it the identifier of the external connection.
4) The OMS generates a response message and sends it to APCS.
5) APCS receives the request processing result.
6) The process ends.

9.1.2. Process “01.00.00.01 Getting a client token by means of the TT GIS”
A diagram of the process for getting a client token for applying to OMS API by means of the
TT GIS can be found in Figure 237.


---

## Page 504


504
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Process “Getting a client token by means of the TT GIS”
Figure 237
Description:
1) APCS forms an authorization request (see section 9.3.2.1) and sends it to TT GIS.
2)  TT GIS verifies the request.
-
If the request contains errors, the TT GIS will register the error in the log;
-
TT GIS generates an error message and sends it to APCS;
-
Then you will be taken to step 5) of the main scenario.
3) If there are no errors, the TT GIS generates a random data string for signing and token
receiving.


---

## Page 505


505
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

4) TT GIS generates a response message and sends it to APCS.
5) APCS receives the request processing result.
-
In case if the authorization request response contains information on the errors occurred,
the process will end.
6) If the authorization request is successfully completed, APCS generates an
authentication token request (see. section 9.3.2.2) and sends it to TT GIS.
7)  TT GIS verifies the request.
-
If the request contains errors, TT GIS will register the error in the log;
-
TT GIS generates an error message and sends it to APCS;
-
Then you will be taken to step 13) of the main scenario.
8) If there are no errors, TT GIS sends a request for verification of the integration decision
installation registration in OMS.
9)  OMS verifies the received information on integration decision installation.
-
If installation of the integration decision is not registered in OMS, OMS will
register an error in the log;
-
OMS generates an error message and sends it to TT GIS;
-
Then you will be taken to step 11) of the main scenario.
10) If verification of data on the installation of the integration decision has been
successful, OMS will register a new token for the installation of the integration decision
and will form a response message and send it to the TT GIS.
11) TT GIS receives the request processing result.
12) TT GIS generates a response message and sends it to APCS.
13) APCS receives the request processing result. If the request has been successfully
completed, APCS will transmit the received token in HTTP header in the “clientToken”
parameter when applying to OMS API. Once the client token has expired, the
procedure for receiving the token shall be repeated.
14) The process ends.

9.1.3. Process “01.00.00.02 Getting a client token by means of MDLP IS”
Diagram of process of the client token receiving by means of MDLP IS can be found in
Figure 238.


---

## Page 506


506
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Process “Getting a client token by means of the MDLP IS”
Figure 238
Description:
1)  APCS forms a request for getting the authentication code (see section 9.3.1.1) and
sends it to IS MDLP.
2)  MDLP IS verifies the request.
-
If request contains errors, MDLP IS will register the error in the log;
-
MDLP IS generates an error message and sends it to APCS;


---

## Page 507


507
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

-
Then you will be taken to step 5) of the main scenario.
3)  MDLP IS will generate the authentication code for token receiving if there are no
errors.
4)  MDLP IS generates a response message and sends it to APCS.
5)  APCS receives the request processing result.
-
In case if the authorization request response contains information on the errors
occurred, the process will end.
6)  If the authorization request is successfully completed, APCS generates the
authentication token request (see. section 9.3.1.2) and sends it to MDLP IS.
7) The MDLP IS verifies the request.
-
If request contains errors, MDLP IS will register the error in the log;
-
MDLP IS generates an error message and sends it to APCS;
-
Then you will be taken to step 13) of the main scenario.
8) If there are no errors, MDLP IS sends a request for verification of the integration
decision installation registration in the OMS.
9)  OMS verifies the received information on integration decision installation.
-
If the installation of integration decision is not registered in OMS, OMS will
register an error in the log;
-
OMS generates an error message and sends it to MDLP IS;
-
Then you will be taken to step 11) of the main scenario.
10) If verification of the data on the installation of the integration decision has been
successful, OMS will register a new token for the installation of the integration decision
and will form a response message and send it to MDLP IS.
11) MDLP IS receives the request processing result.
12) MDLP IS generates a response message and sends it to APCS.
13) APCS receives the request processing result. If request has been successfully
completed, APCS will transmit the received token in HTTP header in the “clientToken”
parameter when applying to OMS API. Once the client token has expired, the
procedure for receiving the token shall be repeated.
14) The process ends.

## Page 508


508
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

9.2.
Registering the installation of the integration decision instance
9.2.1. Method “Requesting registration of installation of the integration decision
instance”
This method is used to send the request for registering the installation of the integration
decision instance to the OMS.
Request on registration of installation of the integration decision instance shall be signed
with GCP certificate.
The circulation participant generates a request, signs it and generates a detached signature
using GCP certificate. The GCP's detached signature is included into HTTP header within X-
Signature parameter, in Base64 encoding. For signature, the data from the message body is
used.
Note: the system does not support attached signature, therefore error and HTTP code
413 are returned when specifying this signature in “X-Signature” parameter.
In this section the <url of API environment to register the installation> is the base address
of the environment where the API is located to register the installation of the integration solution
instance.
The following environment addresses are available to send a request to register the
installation of the integration solution instance:
1. https://suz-integrator.sandbox.crptech.ru
–
new
base
address
of
demo
environment. The following code of integration solution registration is available to
all participants on the demo environment for testing: 4344d884-7f21-456c-981e-
cd68e92391e8;
2. https://suzgrid.crpt.ru:16443 – base address of production environment.
9.2.1.1. Request
Example of request can be found in Figure 239.


---

509
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Accept: application/json
Content-Type: application/json
X-Signature: <Открепленная подпись запроса>
X-RegistrationKey: cdf12109-10d3-11e6-8b6f-0050569977a1

{
  "address": "г.Москва, ул. Ленинские горы, 1",
  "name": "Наименование"
}

9.3.
Getting a client token by means of single authentication
methods
9.3.1. Getting a client token by applying to single authentication methods of MDLP IS
This section describes the methods of MDLP IS API for a GCP with GG “Medicines for
medical use” to receive a token used in applying to OMS API methods (see section 4.4)
In this section, <version> refers to the MDLP IS API version number, <endpoint> refers to
the environment address (endpoint) where MDLP IS API is located.
The following environment addresses are available:
− For resident participants:
− https://api.mdlp.crpt.ru – production environment;
− https://api.sb.mdlp.crpt.ru – Sandbox test environment;
− For non-resident participants:
− https://nrz.api.mdlp.crpt.ru – production environment;
− http://api.mdlp.crpt.ru – production environment;
− https://nrz.api.sb.mdlp.crpt.ru – “Sandbox” test environment.

9.3.1.1. Method for getting the authentication code
This method is used to get the authentication code.


---

513
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

241.

Host: api.sb.mdlp.crpt.ru

  "client_id": "7df0d06f-6510-44fe-a378-76cb53e2605f",
  "client_secret": "49781b3a-19d5-4ad7-b1b6-abb57e598d41",
  "user_id" : "user@example.org",
  "auth_type" : "PASSWORD"
}

where True API can be found.

1) https://markirovka.sandbox.crptech.ru/api/v3/true-api - base address of demo environment
2) https://markirovka.crpt.ru/api/v3/true-api – base address of the production environment.

This method is used for getting the security marker (authentication token) for the OMS. For
receiving the OMS token, the “omsConnection” parameter - unique connection identifier (external
connection) assigned to the registered installation of the integration solution - is added to the
method.

URL
<url of environment V3>/auth/simpleSignIn/{omsConnection}
HTTP header parameters can be found in Table 382.

Table 382 – HTTP header parameters (HTTP Header)
Parameter
Description
Type
Mandatory
Content-type
Content-type: application/json
String
Yes

Request string parameters are given in Table 383.


---

## Page 521


521
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Table 383 – Request String Parameters (Query string parameters)
Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
omsConnection
Unique connection identifier
(external connection) assigned to
the registered installation of the
integration solution.

It is issued while registering the
installation of the integration
solution (see section  9.2).
String
It shall be indicated for
getting a token for
access to OMS API
Note: in the transitional period, obtaining a unique connection identifier (external connection
- omsConnection) will also be available by registering the client device (system) in the OMS user
interface where it will interact through the OMS API.
Request body parameters are given in Table 384.
Table 384 – Request Body Parameters (HTTP Body)
Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
uuid
Unique identifier of signed
random data
String
Yes
data
Random data signed with EQES
of registered GCP in base64
(attached electronic signature)
String
Yes
inn
INN of the goods circulation
participant for which authorization
is required for an individual under
a machine-readable power of
attorney
String
No
unitedToken
Indicator that a single token is
requested as uuid

Possible values:

true — to request a token of
UUID format (single
authentication token)
Boolean
No

9.3.2.2.2 Response
An example of JSON response is shown in Figure 250.

Content-Type: application/json

  "token":"58f908f1-832a-4ad0-93f4-bdafbf8eb55a "
}

Content-Type: application/json

{
  "uuidToken":"123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426655440000",
  "expireDate":"2026-10-10T00:00:00.123Z"
}
Example of JSON response when a token of UUID format is requested
Figure 251

Upon successful completion of request, the server returns HTTP code 200 and a token, or
an error message.
A structure of response to request is given in Table 385.
Table 385 – Format of Response to Request
Field
Description
Type
Mandatory
token
Authentication token. Token is valid
for 10 hours. Parameter to be
specified in case of successful
response
String
No
uuidToken
Authentication token of UUID
format. (single token). It is returned
if unitedToken=true is specified in
the request
String
No
expireDate
Token expiration date and time in
yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss[.SSS]'Z’
format

It is returned only for uuid token
String
No
code
Error code
String
No
error_message Error message
String
No

description

String

## Page 524

RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

10. RECEIPTING 2.0
This section describes the data structures within Receipting 2.0. When receiving a
document from the OMS participant, to ensure integrity and stability of the data, a transaction
start document with information on document receipt depending on its type and destination, is
formed. Then, during processing of the participant’s document in the information system, events,
which contain information on the processing, are formed. Thus, a receipt in Receipting 2.0 is a
document that contains the data of the transaction start document and all events occurring as
part of the transaction.

The receipt structure can be found in Table 386.
Table 386 – Receipt Structure
Attribute
Description
Type
Mandatory
resultDocId
Receipt identifier
String
Yes
resultDocDate
Date and time of receipt update.
Date and time in UNIX format, in
milliseconds
Integer
Yes
sourceDocId
Identifier of the source document - the
document for which the receipt was
formed.
String
Yes
sourceDocDate
Date and time of the source document
- the document for which the receipt
was formed. Date and time in UNIX
format in milliseconds
Integer

Yes
state
Receipt processing status. The value
of the guide “Receipt statuses” (see
Section 5.3.1.17).
String
Yes
code
Numerical code of receipt processing.
The value of the guide “Receipt
statuses” (see Section 5.3.1.17).
Integer
Yes
description
Text description of the receipt
processing result.
String
Yes
workflow
Receipt type.
The value of the guide “Types of
receipts” (see Section 5.3.1.14)
String
No
workflowVersion
Version of the receipt type. Valid
value: 1
Integer
No
details
User data of the receipt
Object
(see Table
387)

No
operations
List of the events on the document
within transaction.
Object
(see Table
388)
No


---

## Page 525


525
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

User data of the receipt can be found in Table 387
Table 387 – User data of the receipt
Parameter
Description
Type
Mandatory
productGroupIds
Identifier of the goods group.  The
value of the guide “Goods groups”
(see Section 5.3.1.16)
Integer
Yes
participantInn
INN of the participant.
String
Yes
serviceProviderId
Service provider identifier.
String
(UUID)
No
txType
Receipt type.
The value of the guide “Types of
receipts” (see Section 5.3.1.14)
String
Yes
startDocDate
Date and time of the source
document processing start (start of
transaction) in UNIX format in
milliseconds.
Integer
Yes
createDocDate
Date and time of the source
document creation in UNIX format
in milliseconds.
Integer
Yes
orderId
Order identifier assigned in the
OMS or MCDN.
String
No
productionOrderId
Production order identifier.
String
No
distributionProviderInn INN of the integrator of the service
provider.
String
No

Structure of the events on document can be found in Table 388.


---

## Page 526


526
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Table 388 – Event Structure
Parameter
Description
Type
Mandatory
operationId
Event identifier
String
Yes
operationType Event type. The value of the guide “Types of
events” (see Section 5.3.1.15)
String
Yes
operationDate Date and time of the event.
Date and time in UNIX format in milliseconds.
Integer
Yes
docId
Identifier of the document related to the receipt
(the document formed for event)
String
No
docDate
Date of the document formed for the event.
Date and time in UNIX format.
Integer
No
docHash
Hash sum of the document formed for event
(sha-256).
String
No
details
User data of the event.
Content depends on the receipt type and event
itself (for structure of each event, see Section
10.1)
Object
No
Note: A docId parameter is not transferred in the receipts for operations with REJECTED
status for the “Medicines for human use” goods group. Errors can be viewed in the MDLP FGIS
user account (in the section "Registry of documents" -> "Outgoing") by operationId number of the
event completed with error.

processed partially (the order was processed successfully for one “gtin” and was not

10.1.1.1.
Event “Order receipt” (CREATE_ORDER_RECEIVED)
The event is formed only after successful signing of the order for all goods groups, except
“Tobacco products”, “Medicines for human use”, and “Nicotine products”. For the goods groups
specified, the event is formed after the order has been received successfully.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 389

10.1.1.2.
Event “Dispatch of order for verification” (CREATE_ORDER_VALIDATION)
The event is formed only after the order is sent successfully for verification. At the same
time:
− If there had been an accident in the MDLP IS at the time of creation of the transaction
start document, the event would also have not been formed for the “Medicines for
human use”
− If the settings of the order check had been turned off for the “Tobacco products”,
“Nicotine products”, “Alternative tobacco products”, “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol
beverages” goods groups at the time of creation of the transaction start document, the
event would also have not been formed for these goods groups.

10.1.1.3.
Event “Order verification result” (CREATE_ORDER_VALIDATION_RESULT)
The event is formed after verification of the order. At the same time:
− If there had been an accident in the MDLP IS at the time of creation of the transaction
start document, the event would also have not been formed for the “Medicines for
human use” goods group;
− If the settings of the order check had been turned off for the “Tobacco products”,
“Nicotine products”, “Alternative tobacco products”, “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol
beverages” goods groups at the time of creation of the transaction start document, the
event would also have not been formed for these goods groups.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 392.

10.1.1.4.
Event
“Dispatch
of
task
to
ER/MER”
(CREATE_ORDER_EMISSION_TASK_SEND)
The event is formed only after the task is sent successfully to ER/MER. The number of
events of this type should be equal to the number of tasks sent to ER/MER. There may be
situations in which the final receipt does not display these events because the event is optional
(in the future it will become mandatory).
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 394.

10.1.1.5.
Event “Saving a suborder received” (CREATE_ORDER_GIS_CREATED)
The event is formed after saving the received suborder in the ES. The number of events
of this type should be equal to the number of tasks sent to ER/MER.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 396.

The events are formed after generation of the automatic report “Generated” or “10300”

equal to the number of tasks sent to ER/MER, except for those that were processed with error
(event CREATE_ORDER_GIS_CREATED has REJECTED status) at the suborder saving stage.

Note: status of the receipt depends on the status of these events:
− If all events of this type are completed with “SUCCESS” status, the receipt will have
“SUCCESS” status.
− If all events of this type are completed with “REJECTED” status, the receipt will have
“FAILED” status.
− If events of this type are both with “REJECTED” and “SUCCESS” statuses, receipt will
have “PARTIALLY” status.

10.1.2.2.
Event
“Dispatch
of
report
metadata
for
verification
in
MDLP”
(REPORT_UTILIZE_VALIDATION)
The event is formed only after successful sending of the report metadata to be verified in
the MDLP IS for the “Medicines for Medical Use” goods group.  If there had been an accident in
the MDLP IS at the time of creation of the transaction start document, the event would have not
been formed.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 402.

Parameter
Description

state

SUCCESS – report has been sent for verification
String Yes

Event
“Results
of
report
metadata
verification
in
MDLP”
(REPORT_UTILIZE_VALIDATION_RESULT)
The event will be formed after the report metadata are verified in the MDLP IS for the
“Medicines for Medical Use” goods group. If there had been an accident in the MDLP IS at the
time of creation of the transaction start document, the event would have not been formed.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 403.

403

of

of

Note: if the event is completed with “REJECTED” status, the receipt will have “FAILED”
status and further events will not be formed.

10.1.2.4.
Event “Dispatch of report to ER/MER” (REPORT_UTILIZE_EMISSION_SEND)
The event is formed taking into account the results of sending of the report to ER/MER.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 404.

The event is formed taking into account the results of sending of the report for processing.

10.1.2.6.
Event “Report processing result” (RNMS_GIS_PROCESSED)

Mandatory
productGroupIds Identifier of the goods group. The value of the guide
“Goods groups” (see Section 5.3.1.16)
Integer No
state
Operation processing status. Available values:
• SUCCESS – processing of the report on gtin
succeeded;
• REJECTED - processing of the report on gtin
failed;
• PARTIALLY - received report on gtin has been
partially processed (the status is not applied
for the rejection report).
String
Yes
description
Text description of the operation processing result.
String
No
processed
Total number of the codes processed successfully in
the report on gtin.
Integer Yes
total
Total number of the MCs in the report on gtin.
Integer Yes
gtin
Goods code
String
Yes

Note: status of the receipt depends on the status of these events:
− If all events of this type are completed with “SUCCESS” status, the receipt will have
“SUCCESS” status.
− If all events of this type are completed with “REJECTED” status, the receipt will have
“FAILED” status.
− If among the events of this type there are events with “REJECTED” and “SUCCESS”
status or among the events of this type there is at least one with “PARTIALLY” status,
the receipt will have “PARTIALLY” status.

10.1.4.1.
Event “MC cancellation” (ANNULMENT_CODES)
The event is formed after the report has been generated by the information system in
case of closing of the order with marking codes with “ACTIVE” status or if MCs in the order are
expired.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 411.

–

ANNULMENT_CODES_EMISSION_SEND
Parameter

10.1.4.3.
Event “Dispatch of report for processing” (RNMS_GIS_ROUTED)
The event is formed taking into account the results of sending of the report for processing.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 406.

10.1.4.4.
Event “Report processing result” (RNMS_GIS_PROCESSED)
The event is formed taking into account the results of the report processing. The number
of events of this type should be equal to the number of unique gtin in the report. The event may
not be formed if the reason for cancellation was expiration of the MCs.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 407.

10.1.5.1.
Event “Receipt of rejection report” (REPORT_DROPOUT_RECEIVED)
The event is formed after the report has been received successfully.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 414.

Table 414 – Structure of the user data of the event REPORT_DROPOUT_RECEIVED
Parameter
Description
Type
Mandatory
quantity
Number of dropped out MCs in the rejection
report
Integer
(int32)
Yes
dropoutReason
Reason for MCs dropout. Guide value of the
guide “Disposal Reasons”.
String
Yes
state
Operation processing status. Valid values:
SUCCESS – report has been received
String
Yes
description
Text description of the operation processing
result.
String
No
omsId
OMS profile identifier
String
(UUID)
Yes
participantId
Identifier of the goods circulation participant
String
No
serviceProviderName Service provider name.
String
No
serviceProviderInn
INN of the Service Provider
String
No
createDocDate
Date and time of the source document
creation in UNIX format in milliseconds.
Integer
(int64)
Yes

10.1.5.2.
Event
“Dispatch
of
report
to
ER/MER/marking
system”
(REPORT_DROPOUT_EMISSION_SEND)
The event is formed taking into account the results of sending of the report to
ER/MER/marking system.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 415.


---

## Page 562


562
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Table
415
–
Structure
of
the
user
data
of
the
event
REPORT_DROPOUT_EMISSION_SEND
Parameter
Description
Type Mandatory
state
Document processing status. Valid values:
• SUCCESS - report has been sent
• REJECTED – report has not been sent.
String Yes
description Text description of the operation processing result. String No

Note: if the event is completed with “REJECTED” status, the receipt will have “FAILED”
status and further events will not be formed.

10.1.5.3.
Event
“Dispatch
of
report
for
processing”
(REPORT_DROPOUT_GIS_ROUTED)
The event is formed taking into account the results of validation and sending of the report
for processing only for the “Tobacco products”, “Nicotine products” and “Alternative tobacco
products” goods groups.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 416.


---

## Page 563


563
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Table
416
–
Structure
of
the
user
data
of
the
event
REPORT_DROPOUT_GIS_ROUTED
Parameter
Description
Type
Mandatory
productGroupIds Identifier of the goods group. The value of the
guide “Goods groups” (see Section 5.3.1.16)
Integer
(int32)
Yes
signINN
Sender’s INN
String
No

Integer
(int64)
No

• REJECTED – report has not passed

Yes

No

Note: if the event is completed with “REJECTED” status, the transaction will have
“FAILED” status and further events will not be formed.

10.1.5.4.
Event “Dispatch of report for processing” (RNMS_GIS_ROUTED)
The event is formed taking into account the results of validation and sending of the report
for processing (for all goods groups except the “Tobacco products”, “Nicotine products” and
“Alternative tobacco products” goods groups).
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 407.

10.1.5.5.
Event “Report processing result” (REPORT_DROPOUT_GIS_PROCCESSED)
The event is formed taking into account the results of the report processing for the
“Tobacco products”, “Nicotine products” and “Alternative tobacco products” goods groups.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 417.


---

## Page 564


564
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

Table
417
–
Structure
of
the
user
data
of
the
event
REPORT_DROPOUT_GIS_PROCESSED
Parameter
Description
Type
Mandatory
productGroupIds Identifier of the goods group. The value of the
guide “Goods groups” (see Section 5.3.1.16)
Integer
(int32)
Yes
state
Operation processing status. Available values:
• SUCCESS – processing of the report on
gtin succeeded;
• REJECTED – processing of the report on
gtin failed;
String
Yes
description
Text description of the operation processing
result.
String
No

Note: status of the receipt depends on the status of these events:
− If all events of this type are completed with “SUCCESS” status, the receipt will have
“SUCCESS” status.
− If all events of this type are completed with “REJECTED” status, the receipt will have
“FAILED” status.
− If events of this type are both with “REJECTED” and “SUCCESS” statuses, receipt will
have “PARTIALLY” status.

10.1.5.6.
Event “Report processing result” (RNMS_GIS_PROCESSED)
The event is formed taking into account the results of the report processing (for all goods
groups except the “Tobacco products”, “Nicotine products” and “Alternative tobacco products”
goods groups). The number of events of this type should be equal to the number of unique gtin
in the report.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 407.

10.1.6.1.
Event “Result of MC issue” (GET_CODES_RESULT)
The event is formed after MCs have been successfully issued.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 418.

Table 418 – Structure of the user data of the event GET_CODES_RESULT
Parameter
Description
Type
Mandatory
state
Operation processing status. Valid
values:
SUCCESS – MCs are issued.
String
Yes

Text description of the operation
processing result.
String
No
products
Goods description
Object
(see Table
419)
Yes
reserveId
Pool identifier
String (UUID)
No

Identifier of the goods circulation
participant
String
No

String
No

INN of the Service Provider
String
No
omsId
OMS profile identifier
String (UUID)
Yes
createDocDate
Date and time of the source document
creation in UNIX format in milliseconds.
Integer (int64)
Yes
sourceRequest
Source of the request for MC issue.
Valid values: UI or API
String
Yes

Description of the structure of “products” parameter is given in Table 419.

Table 419 – Structure of “products” parameter
Parameter
Description
Type
Mandatory
gtin
Goods code
String
Yes
quantity
Number of the MCs issued for
this gtin
Integer
(int32)
Yes

Note: If MCs are issued for a goods group, which does not provide for creation of the
automatically generated report on the MC application or MCs are received by a service provider

## Page 568


568
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

(the order has been created for the service provider), then the transaction will have “SUCCESS”
status and further events will not be formed.

10.1.6.2.
Event “Dispatch of automatically generated report on the MC application”
(GET_CODES_GENERATED_REPORT)
The event is formed taking into account the results of sending of the automatically
generated report to ES.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 420.

Table
420
–
Structure
of
the
user
data
of
the
event
GET_CODES_GENERATED_REPORT
Parameter
Description
Type
Mandatory
state
Document processing status. Valid values:
• SUCCESS – report has been sent
• REJECTED – report has not been sent
String
Yes
description Description
String
No
reserveId
Pool identifier
string (UUID) No

Note: if the event is completed with “REJECTED” status, the receipt will have “FAILED”
status and further events will not be formed.

10.1.6.3.
Event “Dispatch of report for processing” (RNMS_GIS_ROUTED)
The event is formed taking into account the results of sending of the report for processing.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 406.

10.1.6.4.
Event “Report processing result” (RNMS_GIS_PROCESSED)
The event is formed taking into account the results of the processing of the automatically
generated report. The number of events of this type should be equal to the number of unique gtin
in the report.
For a structure of the user data of the event, see Table 407.

10.1.8.1.
Event “Closing of order” (CLOSE_ORDER)

## Page 577


577
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

LIST OF APPLICATION PROGRAMMING INTERFACE CHANGES

Version/
Edition
Date
OMS
version List of Modifications
62
26.05.2025
4.64
The following modifications were introduced:
— Information about the planned transition from UUID v4 to
v8 has been removed (see section 4.4);
— The “productionLineId” parameter has been removed from
the REST request example for the MC aggregation report and
marked as deprecated for the following goods groups:
“Tobacco products” (see section 4.4.10.1.1), “Alternative
tobacco products” (see section 4.4.10.1.3), “Nicotine
products” (see section 4.4.10.1.6);
— The “Pyrotechnic articles” goods group has been added to
the list of goods groups specifying the serial number length
(see section 4.4.1.1);
— A list of attributes for creating and submitting a marking
code emission order has been added for the “Pyrotechnic
articles” goods group (see section 4.4.1.1.48);
— The “Pyrotechnic articles” goods group has been added to
the list of goods groups for sending an MC utilization report
to the OMS (see section 4.4.11);
— A list of the “UtilisationReport” object extensions has been
added for the “Pyrotechnic articles” goods group (see section
4.4.11.1.43);
— Information on the “Pyrotechnic articles” goods group has
been added to the “MC templates” guide (see section
5.3.1.4);
— Information on the “Pyrotechnic articles” goods group has
been added to the “Marking code type” guide (see section
5.3.1.10);
— Information on the “Pyrotechnic articles” goods group has
been added to the “Goods Groups” guide (see section
5.3.1.16);
— A description of the marking code structure and format has
been added for the “Pyrotechnic articles” goods group (see
section 8.48);
— Information on availability of the “REMAINS” value for the
“Engine oils” goods group has been removed from the
description of the “Method of Goods Introduction into
Circulation” guide (see section 5.3.1.1);
— For the “Radio-electronic products. Electronic nicotine
delivery systems” goods group, the end date of the
experiment has been changed for the first payment wave
from May 31, 2026 to August 31, 2026 (inclusive) (see section
4.4.11.1.39).
61
30.04.2026
4.63
The following modifications were introduced:


---

## Page 578


578
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

— For
the
“Tobacco
products”,
“Alternative
tobacco
products”, and “Nicotine products” goods groups, the
requirement for the “productionDate” parameter in the MC
application report has been changed from optional to
conditionally mandatory (see section 4.4.11.1.1, 4.4.11.1.4,
4.4.11.1.6);
— For the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages”
goods group, the requirement for the “alcoholVolume”,
“documentNumber”, “documentDate” parameters in the MC
application report has been changed from optional to
conditionally mandatory (see section 4.4.11.1.7);
— For the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages”
goods group, the note on filling requirements for the
“alcoholVolume”, “documentNumber”, and “documentDate”
parameters in the MC application report has been updated
(see section 4.4.11.1.7);
— Error codes 300, 301, and 302 have been added to the list
of marking code processing result codes for the application
and rejection reports (see section 5.3.1.18).
60
24.04.2026
4.63
The following modifications were introduced:
— The URL of the demo environment with GOST TLS
support has been added to the OMS API methods (see
section 4.4);
— The code structure description has been updated for the
“Radio-electronic products” goods group (see section 8.23);
— Details on the pilot program for the eighth payment group
have been added for the “Medical products” goods group (see
section 4.4.1.1.10);
— The fifth payment group has been added for the “Dairy
products” goods group (see section 4.4.1.1.8);
— The “Clothing items, bed, table, bath and kitchen linens”
goods group has been renamed “Light industry” within the
document;
— For the “Beer, beer-based and low-alcohol beverages”
goods group, a note on specifying the actual ethyl alcohol
content in the MC application report has been added (see
section 4.4.11.1.7);
— For
the
“Tobacco
products”,
“Alternative
tobacco
products”, “Nicotine products” goods groups, a note on
specifying the production date in the MC application report
has been added (see section 4.4.11.1.1, 4.4.11.1.4,
4.4.11.1.6);
— The “expireDate” parameter has been added to the
response of the “Receiving an Authentication Token” method,
a response example for the UUID token format has been
updated (see section 9.3.2.2.2);


---

## Page 579


579
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

— The new optional “expDateConditions” attribute (with
expiration dates and storage condition indexes) has been
added to the description of the application report structure for
the “Confectionery products” goods group, along with usage
notes and a REST request example (see section
4.4.11.1.32);
— A typo has been fixed in the note for the MC order for the
"Radio-electronic products" goods group, regarding the
period of use of the method for releasing goods into
circulation "REAPPLY" (see section 4.4.1.1.23).
59
08.04.2026
4.62
The following modifications were introduced:
— URLs with GOST TLS support have been added to section
“Methods of OMS API”.
58
26.03.2026
4.62
The following modifications were introduced:
— Information about the planned transition from UUID v4 to
v8 has been added (see section 4.4);
— Request rate limits to the OMS-Cloud API have been
updated (see section 4.4);
— For all goods groups, the mandatory status of the
“createMethodType” parameter has been changed; the
parameter has been marked as deprecated in the request
description of the “Create marking code emission order”
method (see sections 4.4.1.1.2 – 4.4.1.1.47);
— For the “Radio-electronic products” goods group, emission
using
the
method
of
introduction
into
circulation,
“Manufacture in the Russian Federation”, has been
discontinued for goods codes of technical range (029) in the
"Create marking code emission order" method (see section
4.4.1.1.23);
— For the “Specialized food products and biologically active
food additives” goods group, information about the launch of
the fourth payment wave has been added to the MC order
note (see section 4.4.1.1.15);
— For the “Semi-finished and frozen food products” goods
group, the application report structure description has been
updated with: a new optional attribute “expDateConditions”
(specifying shelf lives and storage condition indexes), filling
instructions, and a REST request example (see section
4.4.11.1.41);
— For the “Medical products” goods group, information on
how to fill in the parameter when releaseMethodType =
“REMAINS” has been added to the description of the
“producer” attribute (INN of the goods card owner).
Information has also been added to the notes on MC
emission using the method of release of goods into circulation
“Marking of remaining items” (see section 4.4.10.1.9);


---

580
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

value has been added to the description of the “Method of

payment wave (see section 5.3.1.1);

products.

and

of release of goods into circulation “Marking outside
production or import” have also been added (see section
4.4.1.1.40);
— For the “Antiseptic/antibacterial skin cleansers and hand
sanitizers” goods group, information on how to fill in the
parameter when releaseMethodType = “REMAINS” has been
removed from the description of the “producer” attribute (INN
of the goods card owner). Information on a method of release
of goods into circulation “Marking of remaining items” has

581
RU 15861920.620111-04 33 07

— The mandatory status of the "productionDate" parameter
has been changed and a note on how to fill in the parameter
